en.editorNote: Modern life makes us tie self-worth to output, so doing nothing feels like being nothing. This cuts that wire. Good for a friend who feels guilty even on weekends — or read it for yourself.
variations: You're not worthy because of what you did. You're worthy because you're here.
You can do absolutely nothing today and still be a whole person.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Blurt healing quotes April 2026 collection; original writing on non-linear healing

zh-tw.content:
復原不是一條直線。
你可以再從頭開始，
不管已經幾次了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人以為走出低潮就應該一路往前，一旦退步就覺得自己失敗。其實復原本來就是進三步退兩步。適合傳給最近情緒又卡住、覺得自己「怎麼又這樣」的朋友。
variations: 退步不是退場，只是這條路本來就會繞。
你可以重來，這不是失敗，是日常。

en.content:
Healing isn't a straight line.
You're allowed to start again,
no matter how many times it takes.
en.editorNote: We expect recovery to be a one-way climb, so a step back feels like failure. But healing is naturally three steps forward, two steps back. Send to a friend stuck again and frustrated with themselves.
variations: Slipping back isn't dropping out — the road just curves.
You can begin again. That's not failure, that's how this works.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paige Rechtman LMHC self-compassion piece; original writing on treating yourself like a friend

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友說話會那麼兇嗎？
那就先別這樣對自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們對朋友能說「沒關係，下次再來」，對自己卻只有「你怎麼這麼廢」。這句話是一個簡單的提醒：把對外的溫柔，留一份給自己。適合貼在浴室鏡子或筆電上。
variations: 你不會這樣罵朋友，請也不要這樣罵自己。
對自己說話的時候，先想想你會不會這樣對你最在乎的人說。

en.content:
Would you talk to a friend the way you just talked to yourself?
Then don't talk to yourself that way either.
en.editorNote: We tell friends 'it's okay, try again' and tell ourselves 'you're useless.' This is the simple flip: lend yourself some of the kindness you spend on others. Good for a bathroom mirror or laptop sticker.
variations: You wouldn't say that to a friend. Don't say it to yourself.
Before you speak to yourself, ask if you'd say it to someone you love.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling May 2026 Mental Health Awareness piece; original writing on permission to be human

zh-tw.content:
你不用每天都很好。
你只要記得，
不好的時候也還是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 社群上大家都看起來很穩定，所以心情不好的時候會懷疑「只有我這樣嗎」。這句話是在說：低潮的你也是你，不需要遮起來。適合傳給最近在硬撐微笑的朋友。
variations: 心情不好的你，還是值得被擁抱的你。
你可以難過，這不會讓你變成另一個人。

en.content:
You don't have to be okay every day.
Just remember
that the not-okay version is still you.
en.editorNote: Social feeds make everyone look stable, so when you're not, you wonder if it's just you. This says: the low version is still you, no need to hide it. Send to a friend forcing a smile this week.
variations: The sad version of you still deserves a hug.
You're allowed to feel bad — it doesn't turn you into someone else.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Quotena 2026 self-healing collection; original writing on softening instead of pushing

zh-tw.content:
有時候復原不是用力，
是放軟。
是讓自己呼吸、睡覺、發呆，
然後再慢慢回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們以為「努力一點就會好」，但其實有些時候越用力越糟。這句話是給那些一直在自我鞭策的人：你可以先放下鞭子。適合傳給最近在強迫自己振作的朋友。
variations: 走不動的時候，不是再逼一把，是先坐下來。
復原有時候長得像在浪費時間，但那其實是必要的空白。

en.content:
Sometimes healing isn't pushing harder,
it's softening.
It's letting yourself breathe, sleep, stare at nothing,
and slowly come back.
en.editorNote: We assume 'just try harder' fixes it, but sometimes the harder you push, the worse it gets. This is for people stuck in self-flagellation mode: put the whip down first. Send to a friend forcing themselves to bounce back.
variations: When you can't move, the answer isn't another shove — it's sitting down.
Healing can look like wasted time. That blank space is the point.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by CMYK spiritual quotes feature; original writing on small daily survival counting as success

zh-tw.content:
今天有起床、有吃飯、有好好刷牙，
就已經是你能給自己的最好答案。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在很累的時期，把生活基本維持下來其實就很厲害了。這句話幫人重新定義「今天有做到事」，不需要拿到什麼成就。適合傳給最近覺得自己一事無成的朋友。
variations: 你今天有活著、有吃飯，這就是成就。
在低潮裡還能照顧自己，比平常完成十件事更重要。

en.content:
You got up, you ate, you brushed your teeth —
that's already the best answer you could give yourself today.
en.editorNote: In hard seasons, just keeping the basics going is a real win. This redefines 'productive': no trophy required. Send to a friend feeling like they accomplished nothing this week.
variations: You stayed alive and ate today. That counts.
Taking care of yourself through a low patch matters more than ten checked boxes on a good day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling 2026 Mental Health Awareness Month feature; original writing on healing not being linear

zh-tw.content:
復原不是一條直線。
你可以好一點，又退回去，再好一點。
那不是退步，那就是復原本來的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「我明明上週還好好的怎麼又這樣」的人。狀態起伏不代表努力作廢。適合傳給最近又跌進低潮、覺得自己在原地打轉的朋友。
variations: 好一點，再壞一點，這就是復原本人，不是你做錯。
你不是回到起點，是在繞著起點往前走。

en.content:
Healing isn't a straight line.
You get better, then worse, then better again.
That's not relapse — that's just what healing actually looks like.
en.editorNote: For people who say 'I was fine last week, why am I back here?' Up-and-down doesn't undo your progress. Send to a friend who's slipped back into a low patch and feels like they're going in circles.
variations: Better, then worse, then better — that IS recovery, not a failure.
You're not back at square one. You're spiraling forward around it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paige Rechtman LMHC self-compassion collection; original writing on talking to yourself like a friend

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友說的那些溫柔的話，
也說一點給自己聽吧。
你也是值得的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們很容易安慰別人，卻對自己超嚴厲。這句話是一個提醒：把對待別人的標準也用在自己身上。適合傳給很會照顧人但常常忘記照顧自己的朋友。
variations: 如果是朋友這樣，你會罵他嗎？不會吧。那也別這樣對自己。
你給別人的溫柔，記得留一份給自己。

en.content:
All those kind things you say to your friends —
say a few of them to yourself too.
You're worth it as well.
en.editorNote: We comfort other people easily but talk to ourselves like a drill sergeant. This is the reminder: the standard you use for them, use for you. Good for friends who take care of everyone except themselves.
variations: Would you yell at a friend for this? No. So don't yell at yourself.
Save some of that kindness you give other people. You need it too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mental Health Center Kids 2026 quotes feature; original writing on rest as biological need

zh-tw.content:
休息不是獎勵，
是你活著本來就需要的東西。
不用先做完什麼才能休息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「我做完這個再休息」常常變成永遠不休息。這句話打破那種「休息要被允許」的心態。適合傳給總是把休息排在最後的工作狂朋友。
variations: 你不需要把今天的事做完才有資格休息，餓了就吃飯，累了就休息。
休息不是事情做完的結果，是讓事情能繼續做下去的前提。

en.content:
Rest isn't a reward.
It's something you need just because you're alive.
You don't have to earn it.
en.editorNote: 'I'll rest after I finish this' often turns into never resting. This kills the idea that rest needs permission. Send to the workaholic friend who always puts rest last on the list.
variations: You don't need to finish the to-do list before you're allowed to rest. Eat when hungry, rest when tired.
Rest isn't what happens after the work. It's what makes the work possible at all.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Sagebrush Counseling 2026 mental health quotes collection; original writing on integration over restoration

zh-tw.content:
復原不是回到從前那個你，
是把現在的你，連同那些痛過的部分，
一起好好帶著走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人以為「好起來」就是變回受傷前的自己。但其實是學會帶著新版的自己往前。這句話適合傳給經歷過大事正在重新調整的朋友，告訴他們「不一樣不是壞事」。
variations: 你不會變回以前那個你，但會變成一個更懂自己的你。
傷口不是要消失，是要長進你身體裡，讓你變得更完整。

en.content:
Healing isn't going back to who you were before.
It's carrying who you are now — including the parts that hurt —
forward with you.
en.editorNote: People assume 'getting better' means returning to the pre-pain version of themselves. Actually it's learning to carry the new version. Good for friends who've been through something big and are still recalibrating — being different isn't bad.
variations: You won't go back to the old you, but you'll become a you who knows yourself better.
The wound doesn't disappear. It gets folded in, and you become more whole because of it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Holistic Counseling Center 2026 Mental Health Awareness reminders; original writing on permission to slow down

zh-tw.content:
你可以慢下來。
沒有人在追你，是你一直催著自己跑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總覺得「再不做就來不及」的人。其實時間還在，是腦袋裡的鬧鐘從來沒關過。適合貼在工作桌前，或傳給最近一直加班的朋友。
variations: 把腳步放慢一點，世界不會因此停下來等你，但你會開始看見它。
你不是慢，是你終於聽見自己需要喘一口氣。

en.content:
You're allowed to slow down.
No one's chasing you — you've just been chasing yourself.
en.editorNote: For people who always feel 'if I don't do it now, it'll be too late.' Time is still there; it's the alarm in your head that never turned off. Good for a sticky note on your desk, or to send to a friend who's been working late every night.
variations: Slow your pace. The world won't stop for you, but you'll finally start to see it.
You're not slow. You're just finally hearing yourself ask for a breath.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Sagebrush Counseling 2026 healing quotes collection; original writing on non-linear healing

zh-tw.content:
好起來不是一條直線。
今天比昨天難受，不代表你退步了，
只是今天剛好風大一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些因為「又不好過了」而責怪自己的人。療癒從來不是天天進步的，本來就是上上下下。適合在低潮的早晨讀給自己聽。
variations: 復原不是越走越好，是越走越懂得怎麼跟自己相處。
壞日子來的時候不是你退步，是你還在路上。

en.content:
Getting better isn't a straight line.
A harder day doesn't mean you're going backwards —
it just means the wind picked up today.
en.editorNote: For people who beat themselves up when a bad day shows up after a good week. Healing was never about constant progress — it was always going to be up and down. Good to read to yourself on a low morning.
variations: Recovery isn't about getting better every day. It's about getting better at being with yourself.
Bad days arriving doesn't mean you've regressed. It means you're still walking.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by POPBEE 2026 healing Korean drama quotes; original writing on quiet self-kindness

zh-tw.content:
今天的功課只有一個：
用對待最好的朋友的方式，對待你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們總是對別人很溫柔，對自己卻特別嚴格。這句話是一個小提醒：今天先把自己當成那個你最捨不得罵的人。適合早上起床看一眼，或在自我責備時讀給自己聽。
variations: 如果是朋友這樣告訴你，你會抱抱他。那為什麼換成自己，你卻先罵了一句？
對自己溫柔不是奢侈，是基本配備。

en.content:
Today's only assignment:
treat yourself the way you'd treat your closest friend.
en.editorNote: We're endlessly gentle with others and brutally hard on ourselves. This is a small reminder: today, be the person you'd never have the heart to scold. Good to glance at first thing in the morning, or to read when you catch yourself spiraling into self-blame.
variations: If a friend told you this, you'd hug them. So why is your first move on yourself a put-down?
Being kind to yourself isn't a luxury. It's the baseline.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Mental Health Center Kids inspirational quotes; original writing on rest as productive

zh-tw.content:
什麼都不做的那段時間，
你不是在浪費人生，
你是在把自己長回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代人最大的罪惡感就是「我今天什麼都沒做」。但有時候，發呆、躺著、看著天花板才是真正在恢復。適合傳給最近覺得自己「沒生產力」就有罪的朋友。
variations: 你不是一台機器，停下來不是壞掉，是在補回被生活拿走的部分。
什麼都沒做，也是一種把自己撿回來的方式。

en.content:
All that time you spent doing nothing —
you weren't wasting your life.
You were growing yourself back.
en.editorNote: Modern guilt's favorite line: 'I didn't do anything today.' But sometimes zoning out, lying still, staring at the ceiling is the real recovery. Good to send to a friend who feels guilty whenever they're not being 'productive.'
variations: You're not a machine. Stopping isn't breaking — it's putting back what life took out of you.
Doing nothing is one of the ways you put yourself back together.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Banksy 'learn to rest, not to quit' theme; original writing reframing tiredness as signal not weakness

zh-tw.content:
累不是你的問題，
是身體在替你說：
「我需要被照顧一下。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們很習慣把疲憊當成弱點，但其實累是一種訊號，不是缺點。這句話適合傳給最近總是說「我還可以撐」的朋友，提醒他：聽身體的聲音，比硬撐重要。
variations: 身體會比腦袋更早知道你已經到極限了，請相信它。
累的時候不是你不夠堅強，是你已經撐太久了。

en.content:
Being tired isn't a flaw —
it's your body quietly saying,
"I need a little care right now."
en.editorNote: We're trained to treat exhaustion like a weakness, but it's just a signal, not a defect. Send this to a friend who keeps insisting they can push through — a small reminder that listening to your body beats outlasting it.
variations: Your body usually knows you've hit your limit before your brain does. Trust it.
You're not tired because you're weak. You're tired because you've been holding it together for too long.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Chris Germer 'a moment of self-compassion' theme; original writing on micro-kindness adding up

zh-tw.content:
對自己溫柔一點，
不是因為你做得很好，
是因為你已經很努力了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人覺得「要先達標才能對自己好」，但溫柔不需要交換條件。這句話適合放在桌前，提醒自己：努力過的人，本來就值得被善待，不是只有成功的人才有資格。
variations: 你不用先變得更好，才配得到溫柔。
對自己好不需要理由，努力活著就已經夠了。

en.content:
Be kind to yourself —
not because you did everything right,
but because you've been trying so hard.
en.editorNote: Most of us treat self-kindness like a reward we have to earn. It isn't. Tape this somewhere you'll see it: the people who are trying deserve gentleness just as much as the people who are winning. Effort is enough.
variations: You don't have to be better first to deserve kindness.
Being good to yourself doesn't require a reason. Showing up to your life already counts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Blurt Foundation 2026 healing quote roundup; original writing on lightness as part of healing

zh-tw.content:
療癒不一定都很沉重。
看一場無腦的劇、笑出聲、吃一份愛吃的甜點，
身體也在悄悄地把自己拼回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人以為要哭一場、想清楚很多事才算療癒，其實大笑跟放空也是。適合傳給那個一直覺得「我都還沒好好處理情緒」、然後又對自己生氣的朋友。讓自己輕鬆一下，也是在好起來。
variations: 不是只有眼淚算療癒，笑出來也算。
今天讓自己開心一下，不是逃避，是身體在補血。

en.content:
Healing doesn't have to be heavy.
Watching something dumb on TV, laughing out loud, eating your favorite dessert —
your body is quietly putting itself back together too.
en.editorNote: People assume healing means crying it out or having deep realizations. Sometimes it's just letting yourself laugh and zone out. Send this to a friend who keeps beating themselves up for 'not processing things properly.' Letting yourself feel lighter is also part of getting better.
variations: Tears aren't the only thing that counts as healing. Laughing counts too.
Letting yourself have a good time today isn't avoidance — it's your body refilling.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by bohemia.tw 溫暖療癒語錄 collection theme; original writing on emotions being allowed to exist

zh-tw.content:
難過的時候不用急著沒事，
讓情緒待一下，
它待夠了就會自己走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們太常被教「要正面思考」，結果連難過都不敢好好難過。這句話想說的是：情緒不是敵人，趕走它反而會留更久。適合傳給總是逼自己「快點振作」的朋友。
variations: 情緒像客人，請它坐下喝杯茶，它喝完自然會走。
難過不需要時限，準備好再起來就好。

en.content:
When you're sad, you don't have to rush back to "fine."
Let the feeling stay a while —
it'll leave on its own once it's done.
en.editorNote: We're told to think positive so often that we forget how to actually grieve. The thing is, emotions aren't enemies — push them away and they stay longer. For the friend who keeps trying to 'snap out of it' on a deadline.
variations: Feelings are like guests. Offer them a seat, and they'll leave when they're ready.
Sadness doesn't come with a time limit. Get up when you're ready.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mark Nepo 'stronger, gentler, more resilient' theme; original writing on still being whole on hard days

zh-tw.content:
就算今天什麼都做不好，
你還是完整的你，
沒有少掉哪一塊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 低潮的時候很容易覺得自己「壞掉了」，但其實你只是累、只是當天狀態不好，你還是原本那個你。適合傳給最近一直在自我否定的朋友，提醒他：你的價值不是用今天的表現算的。
variations: 狀態不好的那天，你不是變壞了，只是被生活壓扁了一下。
今天做不好沒關係，你不會因此變得不夠好。

en.content:
Even if today went terribly,
you're still whole.
Nothing about you went missing.
en.editorNote: Bad days trick you into believing you're somehow broken. You're not — you're tired, you're off, you're still the same person underneath. For the friend who's been quietly tearing themselves apart lately: your worth isn't calculated from one day's performance.
variations: An off day doesn't mean you got worse. It means life squished you for a bit.
Messing up today doesn't subtract anything from who you are.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mental Health Awareness Month 2026 theme on emotions as messengers; original writing on letting feelings exist without judgment

zh-tw.content:
情緒沒有對錯，
它只是來告訴你一些事，
聽完再請它離開就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人會責怪自己「怎麼又這樣想」「怎麼又生氣了」，但情緒本身不是問題，它只是訊號。適合傳給那種一邊難過一邊罵自己不該難過的朋友，幫他先放下那一層自責。
variations: 感覺來了就讓它說話，說完它自己會走。
你不需要解釋為什麼會有這種感覺，它存在就有它的理由。

en.content:
Feelings aren't right or wrong.
They just came to tell you something.
Hear them out, then let them go.
en.editorNote: So many people pile on a second layer of guilt — being upset, then being upset at themselves for being upset. Feelings aren't the problem; they're signals. Send to a friend who keeps apologizing for having emotions. They get to skip the self-blame round.
variations: When a feeling shows up, let it talk. It'll leave once it's been heard.
You don't owe anyone an explanation for what you feel. It's there for a reason.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by POPBEE 2026 healing K-drama quotes theme on slow pace recovery; original writing on tiny daily progress

zh-tw.content:
今天有起床、有吃飯、有好好睡覺，
這樣就已經很了不起了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 低潮期的時候，光是維持基本生活就要用盡全力，但我們很容易覺得「這算什麼成就」。這句話想告訴你：在很累的日子裡，這些小事就是成就。適合傳給最近撐得很辛苦的朋友，或是自己過得不太好的時候讀一下。
variations: 你今天有照顧好自己最基本的需求，這就值得記一筆。
活下來這件事本身就很費力，你做到了。

en.content:
You got up, you ate something, you slept.
That already counts as a lot.
en.editorNote: On hard days, just keeping the basics going takes everything you have — but we brush it off like it's nothing. It isn't. When things are heavy, those small things are the win. For a friend barely holding it together, or for yourself on a rough one.
variations: You handled the basics today. That deserves credit, not dismissal.
Just staying alive takes effort. You did that. That counts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion theme of speaking to yourself as you would a friend; original writing on inner voice kindness

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友那麼溫柔，
要不要也對自己試試看？
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友失敗了我們會說「沒關係下次再來」，自己失敗了卻會罵自己「為什麼這麼笨」。這個雙重標準其實很傷人。適合傳給那種對別人很好、對自己很嚴格的朋友，或是貼在鏡子旁邊提醒自己。
variations: 朋友失誤你會安慰他，那你失誤的時候，可不可以也這樣安慰你自己？
你說給朋友聽的那些好話，自己也值得聽到。

en.content:
You're so gentle with your friends.
Maybe try that on yourself too?
en.editorNote: When a friend messes up, we say 'it happens, try again.' When we mess up, it's 'what's wrong with you.' That double standard does real damage. For the friend who's endlessly kind to everyone but themselves — or stick it somewhere you'll see it in the mirror.
variations: You'd comfort a friend who messed up. Can you offer yourself that same thing?
The kind things you say to your friends — you deserve to hear them too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by cmykhuang.com spiritual quotes on safe space and presence; original writing on not needing to perform okayness

zh-tw.content:
在我面前，
你不用裝沒事，
累就說累，不舒服就說不舒服。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們對最在乎的人反而最常說「我沒事」，因為不想讓他擔心。但真正親近的人，是希望你能放下那層偽裝的。適合傳給最近一直說沒事但你看得出來不對的朋友，讓他知道在你這裡他不用演。
variations: 你在我這裡可以不用堅強，那是你自己一個人的時候用的。
想說的就說，想哭的就哭，這裡是安全的。

en.content:
With me, you don't have to pretend you're fine.
If you're tired, say so.
If it hurts, say so.
en.editorNote: We hide things hardest from the people we love most, because we don't want to be a burden. But the people closest to you actually want you to drop the act. Send this to a friend who keeps saying 'I'm fine' when you can see they aren't. They can stop performing here.
variations: You can put the brave face down with me. Save it for when you're on your own.
Say it if you need to say it. Cry if you need to cry. You're safe here.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing-quote roundup on letting go vs holding on; original writing on the cost of clinging

zh-tw.content:
放手會痛，
但一直握著只會更痛。
手放開，不代表你不在乎，
只是你決定先照顧自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些明明知道該離開、卻還是放不下的人。放下不是無情，是把自己從那個傷口裡先救出來。適合傳給卡在一段關係或一件事裡很久的朋友，或是寫進自己的日記裡。
variations: 放下不是忘記，是不再讓那件事繼續消耗你。
你可以重視一段過去，又同時選擇向前走，這兩件事不衝突。

en.content:
Letting go hurts.
But holding on hurts longer.
Loosening your grip isn't proof you stopped caring —
it's proof you finally chose yourself.
en.editorNote: For the person who already knows they need to leave, but can't quite open their hand yet. Letting go isn't cold — it's the moment you pull yourself out of the wound. Good for a friend stuck in something old, or for your own journal page.
variations: Letting go isn't forgetting. It's just refusing to let it keep draining you.
You can honor what something meant to you and still choose to walk forward. Those two things don't cancel out.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulness-based healing reflections on resting as a spiritual act; original writing on rest as legitimate

zh-tw.content:
今天什麼都沒完成也沒關係。
好好休息，本身就是一件正在進行的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在這個什麼都要產出、什麼都要打卡的時代，休息常常被當成「浪費時間」。但其實身體在修復、情緒在沉澱的時候，你並沒有停下來，你正在做最重要的事。適合傳給那個一放假就罪惡感很重的朋友。
variations: 你不是在浪費時間，你是在補回前陣子被你燒掉的力氣。
休息不是什麼都不做，是讓自己有空間再長出力氣。

en.content:
It's okay if today held nothing finished.
Resting properly is, in itself, a thing in progress.
en.editorNote: In a culture that demands output and check-ins for everything, rest gets rebranded as 'wasted time.' But when your body is repairing and your feelings are settling, you aren't standing still — you're doing the most important work. Send to a friend who feels guilty the second they sit down.
variations: You're not wasting time. You're refilling what you burned through last month.
Rest isn't doing nothing. It's making room for strength to grow back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 marc&angel reflection on coping with the uncontrollable; original writing on releasing control

zh-tw.content:
你沒辦法控制一切，這不是你的失敗，
是世界本來就比你大很多。
你可以做的，是好好照顧今天的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 焦慮的人最常做的事，就是想用想像力把所有不確定都先預演一次。但這只會更累。這句話是想說：放下不是放棄，是承認自己只是人。適合給那種半夜還在想最壞情況的朋友。
variations: 你不需要掌握全世界，你只需要顧好下一步。
想破頭也不會改變結果，那把今天先過好就好。

en.content:
You can't control everything, and that isn't a failure on your part —
the world is just much bigger than you.
What you can do is take good care of today's version of yourself.
en.editorNote: Anxious people try to pre-live every possible outcome in their heads, hoping it'll help. It only exhausts you more. The point here: letting go isn't quitting, it's accepting that you're only human. For the friend who's still awake at 2am rehearsing the worst case.
variations: You don't need to hold the whole world. Just the next step.
Overthinking won't change the outcome. So try just getting through today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion theme of being a moment's kindness changing a day; original writing on micro-kindness to self

zh-tw.content:
今天對自己溫柔一點點就好。
一杯熱水、一個深呼吸、一句「沒關係」，
都算數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對自己好不是什麼大工程，不用報名什麼課程或買什麼東西。就是在一個普通的時刻，給自己一個不嫌棄的眼神。這句話想告訴你：小小的溫柔，也是真的溫柔。適合傳給最近一直在硬撐的自己或朋友。
variations: 對自己好，不用很盛大，認真喝完一杯水就是一種。
你不需要等到崩潰才照顧自己，今天就可以開始。

en.content:
Just be a little kinder to yourself today.
A warm drink, a deep breath, a quiet 'it's okay' —
that counts.
en.editorNote: Being good to yourself isn't a whole production. You don't need a course or a new purchase. It's the small moment when you choose not to roll your eyes at yourself. The message: small kindness still counts as kindness. For yourself or a friend who's been white-knuckling through the week.
variations: Self-care doesn't have to be big. Actually finishing a glass of water counts.
You don't have to wait for a breakdown to start taking care of yourself. Today works.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 mindfulness resource on non-linear healing; original writing on the right to start over

zh-tw.content:
療癒從來不是一條直線。
你可以倒退、可以停下來、可以再從頭開始，
沒有人會扣你分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人會覺得：「我之前不是已經好一點了嗎？怎麼又跌回來。」然後責怪自己沒有進步。其實情緒就是這樣，沒有畢業典禮。這句話想對一直覺得自己「應該已經好了」的人說：你沒有退步，你只是在練習而已。
variations: 你可以慢慢來，也可以再來一次，沒人在計算次數。
情緒沒有進度條，今天比較難不代表昨天白費了。

en.content:
Healing was never a straight line.
You're allowed to slide back, to pause, to start from the top —
no one is keeping score.
en.editorNote: People keep thinking, 'Wait, wasn't I doing better last week? Why am I here again?' and then beat themselves up over it. But emotions don't hand out graduation certificates. For anyone who feels like they 'should be over it by now': you didn't regress, you're just practicing.
variations: Take your time, start again, do it twice — nobody's counting.
There's no progress bar on feelings. A hard day today doesn't erase a good one yesterday.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 collection of mood quotes on self-acceptance; rewritten for permission-giving tone

zh-tw.content:
你不用一直很好。
難過的時候難過，累的時候休息，
這些都不是你的錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們從小就被訓練要「正向」、「樂觀」、「不要想太多」，搞得負面情緒好像是一種失敗。可是人本來就會有低潮，就像天氣會下雨一樣。這句話是想告訴你：今天心情不好，是被允許的。傳給那種總是先說「我沒事」的朋友。
variations: 情緒不會因為你假裝沒事就消失，它只是排隊在等你。
你不是負能量，你只是今天比較累。

en.content:
You don't have to be okay all the time.
Be sad when you're sad. Rest when you're tired.
None of that is your fault.
en.editorNote: We get trained early on to 'stay positive,' 'look on the bright side,' 'don't overthink it' — like sadness is some kind of personal failure. But humans have low days the way the sky has rainy ones. This one's permission to feel bad today. Send it to the friend who always says 'I'm fine' first.
variations: Pretending you're fine doesn't make feelings leave. They just wait in line.
You're not 'negative.' You're just tired today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by April 2026 healing quotes collection; original writing on integrating past selves

zh-tw.content:
你不需要變回以前的自己。
那個人已經盡力把你帶到這裡了，
剩下的，由現在的你慢慢長出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 走過一些事情之後，人是會變的。有人會懷念以前比較天真、比較有活力的自己，覺得「我回不去了」。但你不需要回去。以前的你不是更好版本，是過去版本。這句話想說的是：你現在的樣子也值得被認識。適合給經歷過一些變動還在找自己的朋友。
variations: 你不是壞掉了，你只是長出了新的形狀。
回不去不是壞事，那代表你已經走了很遠。

en.content:
You don't need to become the old version of you.
That person did their best to get you here —
the rest is for the current you to grow into.
en.editorNote: After going through stuff, people change. Some folks miss the old version of themselves — more naive, more energetic — and think 'I can't get back there.' You don't have to. The old you wasn't a better you, just an earlier one. The current you deserves to be known too. For a friend still finding themselves after a big shift.
variations: You're not broken. You just grew into a different shape.
Not being able to go back isn't a loss. It means you walked pretty far.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Mental Health Awareness Month gentle reminders; rewritten on slowness as a form of strength

zh-tw.content:
走得慢，不代表你輸了。
有些風景，本來就是要慢慢看才看得到，
包括你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看身邊的人好像都在進度上：升遷、結婚、買房、出國，自己一個人站在原地，會懷疑是不是哪裡出問題。其實人生不是賽跑，是散步。慢慢看自己的人，才會真的認識自己。傳給最近在跟別人比、然後比到很累的朋友。
variations: 不是每個人都需要跑，能走完的人也很厲害。
你不是落後，你只是用自己的節奏在前進。

en.content:
Walking slowly doesn't mean you lost.
Some views are meant to be taken in slowly —
including the one of yourself.
en.editorNote: When everyone around you seems to be checking boxes — promotion, marriage, house, moving abroad — and you're standing still, it's easy to wonder what's wrong with you. But life isn't a race, it's a walk. The people who look at themselves slowly are the ones who actually get to know themselves. For the friend who's tired from comparing.
variations: Not everyone needs to run. Finishing on foot is impressive too.
You're not behind. You're moving at your own tempo.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Mental Health Awareness Month gentle reminders; rewritten on healing being non-linear

zh-tw.content:
復原不是一條直線。
你可以今天好一點，明天又退回原地，
然後後天再重新開始一次。
這不叫退步，這叫人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 覺得自己「明明上週狀態還不錯，怎麼今天又掉下去」的人最需要這句。心理的復原本來就會反覆，不是 RPG 經驗值只進不退。傳給最近狀態起起伏伏、然後自責的朋友。
variations: 走兩步退一步，最後還是往前走了一步。
你不是回到原點，你只是又繞了一圈，這次熟悉路了。

en.content:
Healing isn't a straight line.
You can feel okay today, slide back tomorrow,
and start over the day after.
That's not regression. That's being human.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps thinking 'I was fine last week, why am I back here?' Recovery loops — it's not an XP bar that only goes up. Send to a friend whose mood has been up and down and who's beating themselves up about it.
variations: Two steps forward, one step back — still net forward.
You're not back at zero. You just walked the loop again, and this time you know the path.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by healingbrave.com 'quotes about rest'; rewritten on permission to do less today

zh-tw.content:
今天可以小小的、誠實的、夠用就好。
不用很厲害，不用感人，
撐過今天本身就是一件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給總是覺得「今天好像沒做什麼」就有罪惡感的人。生產力不是人的全部，活著本來就在消耗能量，撐過低潮的日子比衝刺的日子更耗電。傳給那種週末躺一天就會內疚的朋友。
variations: 今天的標準可以低一點，明天再說。
什麼都沒做的一天也是你的一天，不用補償誰。

en.content:
Today can be small, honest, and just enough.
It doesn't have to be impressive or meaningful —
getting through it is the thing.
en.editorNote: For people who feel guilty when they 'didn't do much' today. Productivity isn't your whole identity, and staying afloat on a hard day costs more energy than a sprint day. Send to the friend who can't lie on the couch for one weekend without guilt-spiraling.
variations: Lower the bar today. Tomorrow is its own day.
A day where you did nothing is still yours. You don't owe anyone a productive version of it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by vocus.cc '《療癒，從感受情緒開始》'; rewritten on emotions as messengers not problems

zh-tw.content:
情緒不是來找你麻煩的，
它是來告訴你：
有個地方需要被你看見了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對那種一有負面情緒就想趕快壓下去、覺得自己不該這樣的人。情緒像門鈴，響了表示有事，硬是不開門，它只會按得更大聲。傳給最近一直壓抑自己、然後爆炸的朋友，或自己也讀一讀。
variations: 難過、生氣、不安，這些都不是病，是訊號。
你不用打敗情緒，只要聽聽它想說什麼。

en.content:
Feelings aren't here to mess with you.
They're here to tell you:
something inside needs your attention.
en.editorNote: For people who try to shut down any negative feeling the moment it shows up. Emotions are like a doorbell — if you don't answer, it just rings louder. Send to a friend who's been bottling things up until it bursts out, or read it for yourself.
variations: Sadness, anger, anxiety — these aren't malfunctions. They're signals.
You don't have to defeat your feelings. Just listen to what they're trying to say.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Mental Health Center Kids 'uplifting quotes'; rewritten on being enough as you are

zh-tw.content:
你不用變得更好才值得被愛。
你現在這個樣子，已經夠了。
那些要你「再進步一點」才願意靠近的人，
本來就不是要陪你的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直覺得「等我瘦下來」「等我升職」「等我變得更成熟」才敢被人看見的朋友。值得這件事不是你賺來的，是你本來就有的。傳給總是在自我改造、然後忘了喘氣的朋友。
variations: 你不是還沒達標的版本，你就是完整的人。
等你變更好才愛你的，那是條件，不是愛。

en.content:
You don't have to become better to deserve love.
The version of you right now is already enough.
Anyone who's waiting for you to 'improve' before they show up
was never going to stay anyway.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps saying 'once I lose the weight' / 'once I get the promotion' / 'once I'm more put together' before they'll let anyone close. Worthiness isn't something you earn — it came with you. Send to the friend who's always renovating themselves and forgetting to breathe.
variations: You're not a draft waiting to be approved. You're a whole person already.
Love that arrives only after you've 'fixed' yourself isn't love — it's a condition.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Blurt It Out April 2026 healing quotes collection; rewritten on healing not being linear

zh-tw.content:
療癒不是一條直線。
有時候你以為走了三步，
結果又退回一步，
沒關係，那一步也是路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我上個月明明已經好多了，怎麼又變這樣」的人。復原從來不是一直往前，會有反覆才正常。傳給最近因為情緒又掉下去、覺得自己很糟的朋友。
variations: 好轉本來就有起有落，不是你出了問題。
退回去那一步不是失敗，是讓你看清楚下一步往哪走。

en.content:
Healing isn't a straight line.
Sometimes you think you've taken three steps forward
and then slide back one —
that's okay. That step counts too.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps thinking 'I was so much better last month, why am I like this again?' Recovery was never supposed to move in one direction — looping back is part of it. Send to a friend who slipped emotionally this week and is being hard on themselves.
variations: Getting better has its ups and downs. That doesn't mean something's wrong with you.
Sliding back one step isn't failure. It's how you see where the next step needs to go.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling 2026 Mental Health Awareness Month post; rewritten on rest as birthright not reward

zh-tw.content:
休息不用先賺到。
你不需要把今天做到完美，
才有資格躺下來。
累了就休息，這件事不用理由。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一定要把事情做完才敢休息、結果永遠在做事的人。休息不是獎品，是身體本來就需要的東西。傳給總是把自己排在最後一個的朋友，或自己默默讀。
variations: 你不用先有產出，才有資格被善待。
今天沒做完沒關係，你不是機器。

en.content:
You don't have to earn rest.
You don't need to make today perfect
before you're allowed to lie down.
Tired is reason enough.
en.editorNote: For people who can only let themselves rest after they've finished everything — which means they never actually rest. Rest isn't a reward; it's something your body needs by default. Send to the friend who always puts themselves last, or just read it quietly to yourself.
variations: You don't have to be productive first to deserve kindness.
Not finishing today is okay. You're not a machine.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by CMYK Huang 12 healing spiritual quotes; rewritten on tiny progress still counting

zh-tw.content:
今天只比昨天好一點點，
那也算數。
不用每一步都驚天動地，
往前就好，哪怕只是一公分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「沒有大改變就等於沒進步」的人。生活大多時候不會給你戲劇性的高光，就是一天比一天好一點點地累積起來。傳給最近覺得自己原地踏步、其實已經悄悄往前走的朋友。
variations: 進步不一定要看得出來，你自己感覺到就夠了。
一公分也是距離，別小看自己今天的努力。

en.content:
Today is just a little better than yesterday —
that still counts.
Not every step has to be dramatic.
Forward is forward, even if it's only a centimeter.
en.editorNote: For people who think 'if it's not a big change, it doesn't count.' Real life rarely hands you cinematic breakthroughs — it's mostly one slightly-better day stacked on another. Send to the friend who feels stuck but has quietly been moving the whole time.
variations: Progress doesn't have to be visible. If you can feel it, that's enough.
A centimeter is still distance. Don't underestimate what today took from you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paige Rechtman LMHC self-compassion quotes; rewritten on talking to yourself like a friend

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友從來不會說的那些話，
為什麼一直對自己說？
換個語氣，跟自己講話溫柔一點，
你也是值得被好好對待的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對別人超有耐心、對自己卻很兇的人。我們會跟朋友說「沒事，慢慢來」，卻會跟自己說「你怎麼這麼廢」。試著把對朋友的那套說法搬回來給自己用。傳給總是在罵自己的朋友。
variations: 如果朋友這樣講自己，你會心疼，那你也該心疼自己。
對自己兇沒有讓你變更好，只有讓你更累。

en.content:
The things you'd never say to a friend —
why do you keep saying them to yourself?
Change the tone. Speak to yourself a little softer.
You're also someone who deserves to be treated kindly.
en.editorNote: For people who are endlessly patient with everyone else and brutal with themselves. We tell friends 'it's okay, take your time,' then tell ourselves 'why are you so useless.' Try borrowing the friend voice and pointing it inward. Send to the friend who's always beating themselves up.
variations: If a friend talked about themselves the way you talk about you, it'd break your heart. Let it break your heart for yourself too.
Being harsh with yourself never made you better. It only made you more exhausted.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Mental Health Center Kids quotes collection; rewritten on healing not requiring you to perform recovery

zh-tw.content:
你不需要每天都看起來「在康復」。
有些天就只是過了，沒有什麼進展，也沒有什麼倒退，
這也是復原的一部分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得自己「最近沒在好轉」就有罪惡感的人。療癒不是一直要有故事可講，平淡的日子也算數。傳給那種總是在問自己「我今天做了什麼讓自己變好」的朋友。
variations: 不是每一天都要有進度條，活著本身就是進度。
你不用每天都進步，只是繼續存在就已經很厲害了。

en.content:
You don't have to look like you're 'recovering' every day.
Some days just pass — no progress, no regression —
and that's part of recovery too.
en.editorNote: For people who feel guilty when they're 'not getting better fast enough.' Healing doesn't owe you a storyline every day; flat days count too. Send to the friend who keeps asking 'what did I do today to feel better?'
variations: Not every day needs a progress bar. Being here counts.
You don't have to improve daily. Continuing to exist is already a lot.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paige Rechtman LMHC 10 powerful self-compassion quotes; rewritten on giving yourself the kindness you give others

zh-tw.content:
你那麼有耐心對別人，
卻常常忘了把這份耐心，留一點給自己。
你也是值得被你自己好好對待的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對朋友超溫柔、對自己卻很沒耐心的人。我們會勸別人「沒關係慢慢來」，卻會對自己說「怎麼還沒做完」。把那份溫柔轉個方向，自己也接得起來。傳給總是先照顧別人才照顧自己的朋友。
variations: 對自己有耐心，不是寵壞自己，是維持自己。
你給朋友的那種溫柔，你也值得收到一份。

en.content:
You're so patient with everyone else,
and somehow you keep forgetting to save some of that patience for you.
You're also someone who deserves to be treated well by you.
en.editorNote: For people who are endlessly gentle with friends but ruthless with themselves. We tell others 'it's okay, take your time' while telling ourselves 'why aren't you done yet?' Turn that softness around — you can catch it too. Send to the friend who always takes care of everyone else first.
variations: Being patient with yourself isn't spoiling yourself. It's maintenance.
The gentleness you give your friends — you deserve a share of it too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling Mental Health Awareness Month 2026 gentle reminders; rewritten on permission to not be okay

zh-tw.content:
「我今天不太好」這句話，
不需要解釋、不需要道歉、也不需要立刻被解決。
說出來本身，就已經是照顧自己了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種習慣秒回「我沒事」的人。承認自己不好，不代表要把整段故事交代清楚，也不是要對方馬上想辦法。光是說出口，就有減壓的力量。適合在自己快撐不住的時候提醒自己，也可以傳給總是說自己很好的朋友。
variations: 不舒服不需要附上原因清單，難過就是難過。
說出來不是麻煩別人，是給自己一個喘氣的機會。

en.content:
Saying 'I'm not doing great today'
doesn't need an explanation, an apology, or an immediate fix.
Saying it out loud is already a form of self-care.
en.editorNote: For people whose reflex answer is 'I'm fine.' Admitting you're not okay doesn't mean you owe anyone the full story, and the other person doesn't have to solve it. Just speaking it lifts a bit of weight. Read it to yourself when you're close to the edge, or send to the friend who always insists they're good.
variations: You don't need to attach a list of reasons to feeling bad. Sad is sad.
Speaking it isn't burdening anyone. It's giving yourself a chance to breathe.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulnessexercises.com healing quotes collection; rewritten on carrying your past without letting it lead

zh-tw.content:
你不用把過去全部忘掉，才能往前走。
那些事都可以跟著你，
只是它們不再走在你前面帶路了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種以為「沒完全放下就不算走出來」的人。記得不是被困住，遺忘也不是治癒的條件。重點是誰在做決定。適合給那種一直在自責「為什麼我還會想到那件事」的朋友，或自己重複翻舊帳的時候讀。
variations: 記得跟困住，不是同一件事。
你不用刪掉過去，只要不再讓它替你選路。

en.content:
You don't have to forget your past to move forward.
It can all come with you —
it just doesn't get to walk in front of you anymore.
en.editorNote: For people who think 'if I haven't fully let go, I'm not really over it.' Remembering isn't the same as being stuck, and forgetting isn't a condition for healing. The point is who's in charge. Good for a friend who keeps beating themselves up for 'still thinking about it,' or for those late-night replays you put yourself through.
variations: Remembering and being stuck aren't the same thing.
You don't have to delete the past. You just don't have to let it pick the road anymore.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Sagebrush Counseling 2026 mental health quotes collection; rewritten on healing being allowed to be uneventful

zh-tw.content:
你的療癒不一定要有故事可以講。
沒有頓悟、沒有轉折、沒有重生，
只是一天一天活下來，
這也是很完整的一種好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得自己「好像沒在好轉」就有罪惡感的人。社群上的療癒故事都很戲劇化，但真實的復原大多是默默的、無聊的、沒什麼可以發限動的。傳給那種總覺得自己復原進度太慢的朋友。
variations: 沒有大徹大悟也算好起來。
你不用活成一則勵志貼文，平淡也是真的。

en.content:
Your healing doesn't have to come with a story.
No epiphany, no plot twist, no rebirth —
just getting through one day at a time.
That's already a whole kind of okay.
en.editorNote: For people who feel guilty when their recovery isn't 'going somewhere.' Healing stories online are dramatic; real healing is mostly quiet, boring, and not very postable. Send to the friend who keeps thinking they should be further along by now.
variations: Getting better doesn't require a breakthrough moment.
You don't have to live like an inspirational post. Ordinary is real too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling 2026 Mental Health Awareness Month gentle reminders; rewritten on boundaries as care not punishment

zh-tw.content:
你設下的界線，不是在推開誰，
是在保護那個一直在硬撐的你。
讓人靠近之前，你要先有力氣站著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一說「不」就有罪惡感、覺得自己很冷漠的人。界線不是攻擊別人，是讓自己不要被消耗到剩骨頭。傳給那種總是答應太多、然後一個人偷偷崩潰的朋友。
variations: 拒絕不是傷害別人，是先不傷害自己。
你不用為了不讓別人失望，就把自己累到散架。

en.content:
The boundaries you set aren't pushing anyone away.
They're protecting the version of you who's been holding it together for too long.
You have to be able to stand before anyone can lean on you.
en.editorNote: For people who feel guilty the moment they say 'no' and worry they're being cold. Boundaries aren't an attack — they're how you stop being scraped down to the bone. Send to the friend who says yes to everything and then quietly falls apart alone.
variations: Saying no isn't hurting anyone. It's not hurting yourself first.
You don't have to wear yourself down to nothing just to keep someone happy.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mental Health Center Kids 100 powerful quotes 2026; rewritten on small acts of self-care being the real work

zh-tw.content:
今天有好好喝水、有去洗澡、有出門曬到太陽，
這些不是「應該做的小事」，
是你正在認真照顧自己的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「這些事誰不會做」、所以從來不肯給自己功勞的人。在很低潮的日子，光是把基本生活維持住就要用掉很多力氣，這是真的努力。適合在自己又開始覺得「今天什麼都沒做」的時候讀。
variations: 把自己照顧到能正常運轉，已經是一件很厲害的事。
你今天做的那些小事，加起來就是你還在好好活著。

en.content:
You drank water today, you took a shower, you stepped outside and got some sun.
These aren't 'basic things you should be doing.'
They're proof you're actually taking care of yourself.
en.editorNote: For people who refuse to give themselves any credit because 'everyone does these things.' On low days, just keeping your basic life running takes a real amount of energy — that is effort. Good to read when you start telling yourself 'I did nothing today.'
variations: Keeping yourself functional is already an achievement.
All those small things you did today add up to: you're still here, taking care of you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulnessexercises.com healing quotes collection; rewritten on softness being a strength not a flaw

zh-tw.content:
你願意對人溫柔，不代表你天真。
在這個容易變硬的世界裡，
還願意保持柔軟，這本身就是一種勇敢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種被人說「太善良會吃虧」、開始懷疑自己是不是要變得更冷一點的人。溫柔不是弱點，是經歷過很多還願意這樣對人。傳給那種最近被傷過、但還在掙扎要不要繼續對人好的朋友。
variations: 選擇保持柔軟，比變硬更需要力氣。
你不用為了保護自己，就把心關起來。

en.content:
Being gentle with people doesn't make you naive.
In a world that hardens you so easily,
staying soft is its own kind of brave.
en.editorNote: For people who've been told 'you're too kind, you'll get hurt' and have started wondering if they need to grow colder. Gentleness isn't weakness — it's what you choose after going through plenty and still being willing to treat people well. Send to the friend who's been hurt recently and is fighting the urge to shut down.
variations: Choosing to stay soft takes more strength than going hard.
You don't have to lock your heart up just to protect it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paige Rechtman LMHC self-compassion quotes; rewritten on tomorrow not needing to be perfect, only possible

zh-tw.content:
明天不用是新的開始，
不用比今天厲害、不用讓你脫胎換骨。
它只要是「明天」就夠了，
你只要記得睡覺、然後醒來，就可以了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種每天睡前都在逼自己「明天一定要不一樣」、結果壓力大到睡不著的人。明天不需要承擔那麼多，它只要來就好。傳給那種對明天的期待已經變成負擔的朋友，或自己讀一讀。
variations: 明天不用驚天動地，只要它有來就好。
把明天還給明天，今晚的你先好好睡。

en.content:
Tomorrow doesn't have to be a new beginning.
It doesn't have to be better than today or turn your life around.
It just has to be tomorrow — that's enough.
All you have to do is go to sleep and wake up.
en.editorNote: For people who lie in bed every night promising themselves 'tomorrow will be different,' and end up too anxious to sleep. Tomorrow doesn't have to carry that much. It just has to show up. Send to the friend whose anticipation of tomorrow has turned into a weight, or read it for yourself.
variations: Tomorrow doesn't have to be dramatic. It just has to arrive.
Give tomorrow back to tomorrow. Tonight, you just rest.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paige Rechtman LMHC self-compassion quotes; rewritten on comparison quietly draining your joy

zh-tw.content:
你不是過得不好，
你只是看別人的限時動態看太多了。
關掉螢幕，回到自己的生活，
你會發現它其實沒那麼糟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一打開社群媒體就開始覺得自己一事無成的人。別人發出來的都是精選過的高光，你卻拿來跟自己最累的日常比，當然會輸。傳給那種最近一直在跟人比較、越比越焦慮的朋友。
variations: 別人的精選輯，不是你該拿來打分數的標準。
把眼睛從別人身上收回來，你的生活才看得清楚。

en.content:
It's not that your life is going badly —
you've just been watching too many of other people's stories.
Close the screen, come back to your own life,
and you'll find it's not actually that bad.
en.editorNote: For people who open social media and instantly feel like they've accomplished nothing. What others post is a curated highlight reel, and you're comparing it to your most tired, ordinary days — of course you lose. Send to the friend who's been comparing themselves into an anxious spiral.
variations: Someone else's highlight reel isn't the standard you should grade yourself by.
Pull your eyes back from everyone else, and your own life finally comes into focus.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mental Health Center Kids 2026 mental health quotes; rewritten on reaching out not being a burden

zh-tw.content:
你不是在麻煩別人，
你只是讓在乎你的人有機會出現在你身邊。
讓人幫你，也是一種信任。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種寧願自己扛到崩潰、也不想開口的人。你總覺得求助是負擔，但對真正在乎你的人來說，被你需要其實是一種被信任。適合在快撐不住、卻一直找不到開口理由的時候讀。
variations: 願意被幫忙，跟願意幫人一樣，都是溫柔。
你不用什麼都自己來，這個世界不是只有你一個人在。

en.content:
You're not being a burden —
you're giving the people who care about you a chance to show up.
Letting someone help you is a kind of trust, too.
en.editorNote: For people who'd rather carry it until they collapse than say a word. You keep thinking asking for help is a burden, but to the people who genuinely care, being needed by you actually feels like being trusted. Good to read when you're barely holding on but can't find a reason to speak up.
variations: Being willing to be helped is as gentle as being willing to help.
You don't have to do all of it alone. You're not the only one in this world.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulnessexercises.com healing quotes collection; rewritten on letting a bad day stay just one day

zh-tw.content:
今天很糟，沒關係，
它就只是今天而已。
不要把這一天的壞，
貼到你整個人生上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一天不順就覺得「我這個人完蛋了」的人。一個爛日子不等於爛人生，它就只是日曆上的其中一格。傳給那種最近因為一件事不順、就全盤否定自己的朋友，或自己睡前讀一讀。
variations: 壞掉的是今天，不是你。
把今天結束掉，明天它就管不到你了。

en.content:
Today was rough — that's okay.
It's just today.
Don't take the bad of one day
and paste it onto your whole life.
en.editorNote: For people who have one off day and decide 'I'm a total failure.' A bad day isn't a bad life — it's just one square on the calendar. Send to the friend who let one setback turn into a full self-takedown, or read it to yourself before bed.
variations: What broke today was the day, not you.
Let today end, and tomorrow it won't have any say over you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling 2026 gentle reminders; rewritten on it being okay to outgrow who you used to be

zh-tw.content:
你跟以前不一樣了，這不是壞事。
人本來就會變，
你只是長成了現在更適合你的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種因為「我變了」「我跟以前不像」而感到罪惡或失落的人。改變不是背叛過去的自己，是你一直在往前走的證明。傳給那種正在經歷某種轉變、卻有點捨不得過去的朋友。
variations: 你不是變了，你是長大了。
舊版本的你完成了它的任務，可以好好放下了。

en.content:
You're different from who you used to be — and that's not a bad thing.
People are supposed to change.
You've just grown into a version that fits you better now.
en.editorNote: For people who feel guilty or wistful because 'I've changed' or 'I'm not who I used to be.' Changing isn't betraying your old self — it's proof you've kept moving forward. Send to the friend who's going through some kind of shift but feels reluctant to let the old version go.
variations: You didn't change. You grew up.
The old version of you finished its job. You're allowed to set it down.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by CMYK Huang healing spiritual quotes; rewritten on rest being allowed even when nothing is wrong

zh-tw.content:
你不用等到生病、崩潰、撐不下去，
才允許自己休息。
累了就可以停，這件事不需要任何理由。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總要等到身體出狀況、才肯讓自己喘口氣的人。休息不是壞掉之後的維修，是平常就該有的保養。傳給那種把自己逼到極限、卻覺得「我又沒怎樣」的朋友。
variations: 你不用先壞掉，才有資格被修理。
想休息本身，就已經是足夠的理由了。

en.content:
You don't have to wait until you're sick, breaking down, or completely out of road
before you let yourself rest.
Tired is reason enough to stop. You don't need to justify it.
en.editorNote: For people who only let themselves breathe once their body finally gives out. Rest isn't the repair you do after you've broken — it's the upkeep you're supposed to do all along. Send to the friend who pushes themselves to the limit while insisting 'I'm fine, nothing's wrong.'
variations: You don't have to break first to deserve being taken care of.
Wanting rest is already a good enough reason on its own.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by innertune.com healing quotes on worth and timelines; rewritten on not measuring yourself against other people's pace

zh-tw.content:
別人走得比你快，不代表你慢。
你們根本不是在同一條路上，怎麼比？
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一打開社群就忍不住跟別人比進度的人。每個人的起點、行李、路況都不一樣，比較這件事從一開始就不成立。適合在覺得自己「落後了」而焦慮的時候讀。
variations: 你的時區是你的，別人準時不代表你遲到。
花有花開的季節，你只是還沒到。

en.content:
Someone moving faster than you doesn't mean you're slow.
You're not even on the same road — so what is there to compare?
en.editorNote: For people who can't help measuring their progress against others the second they open social media. Everyone starts in a different place, carries different baggage, walks different terrain — the comparison was never fair to begin with. Good to read when you feel 'behind' and it's making you anxious.
variations: Your timezone is yours. Someone else being on time doesn't make you late.
Every flower blooms in its own season. Yours just hasn't come yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by ybgr.org emotional healing theme 'we have to feel it to heal it'; rewritten on letting yourself feel before fixing

zh-tw.content:
你得先感覺到，才有辦法好起來。
一直繞過那個痛，它只會在原地等你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種習慣把情緒壓下去、假裝沒事繼續往前的人。逃避不會讓痛消失，只會讓它換個時間再來。適合在又一次假裝沒事、卻覺得心裡卡卡的時候讀。
variations: 繞過去的傷，總有一天會繞回來。
願意去感覺，本身就是復原的開始。

en.content:
You have to feel it before you can heal it.
Keep walking around the pain, and it'll just wait for you right where you left it.
en.editorNote: For people who push feelings down and pretend they're fine so they can keep moving. Avoiding doesn't make the pain leave — it just reschedules it. Good to read on a day when you brushed something off but it's still stuck in your chest.
variations: The hurt you walk around always finds its way back eventually.
Being willing to feel it is already the start of healing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulnessexercises.com on rest being a precondition for healing; rewritten on stopping as part of the work, not the opposite of it

zh-tw.content:
身體和心都有自己修復的能力，
前提是你願意先讓它們停下來。
休息不是浪費時間，是復原的一部分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「停下來就是落後」、連喘口氣都有罪惡感的人。傷口需要靜止才會癒合，人也一樣。適合在累到不行卻還逼自己撐著的時候讀。
variations: 你不是在偷懶，你是在給自己時間長好。
停下來，不是退步，是讓修復有空間發生。

en.content:
Your body and mind know how to repair themselves —
but only if you let them stop first.
Rest isn't wasted time. It's part of recovering.
en.editorNote: For people who feel like pausing means falling behind, who feel guilty even catching their breath. A wound needs stillness to close, and so do you. Good to read when you're running on empty but still forcing yourself to push through.
variations: You're not slacking off. You're giving yourself time to mend.
Stopping isn't going backward. It's making room for the repair to happen.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by innertune.com 'healing doesn't always look like progress' theme; rewritten on healing sometimes looking like softening and starting again

zh-tw.content:
療癒不一定看起來像在進步。
有時候它只是：好好呼吸、好好休息、
讓自己軟下來，然後再重新開始一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「沒有明顯變好就是沒進展」的人。復原不是一條往上的直線，很多時候它安靜到你以為自己在原地。適合在覺得自己卡住、好像怎麼努力都沒用的時候讀。
variations: 看起來沒在動，不代表裡面沒有在長。
重新開始一次，本身就是一種前進。

en.content:
Healing doesn't always look like progress.
Sometimes it just looks like breathing, resting,
letting yourself soften — and beginning again.
en.editorNote: For people who think 'no visible improvement means no progress.' Recovery isn't a line that only goes up — it's often so quiet you assume you're stuck. Good to read when you feel stalled, like nothing you do is working.
variations: Looking still on the outside doesn't mean nothing is growing inside.
Starting over one more time is its own kind of moving forward.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by marieclaire.com.tw 療癒金句 on being your own warmth; rewritten on becoming the person who catches yourself

zh-tw.content:
你不一定每次都有人接住你，
但你可以慢慢練習，成為那個會接住自己的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總是在等別人來救、卻常常落空的人。最穩的依靠，是你終於學會在跌倒時先伸手抱住自己。適合在覺得孤單、沒人懂的時候讀給自己聽。
variations: 別人不一定在，但你可以一直都在。
先學會接住自己，往後就沒那麼怕跌倒了。

en.content:
There won't always be someone there to catch you.
But you can slowly practice becoming the person who catches yourself.
en.editorNote: For people who keep waiting to be rescued and keep getting let down. The steadiest support is finally learning to wrap your own arms around yourself when you fall. Good to read to yourself on a lonely night when no one seems to get it.
variations: Others might not always be there — but you can be.
Once you learn to catch yourself, falling stops being so scary.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling 2026 'healing is not linear' theme; rewritten on progress not being a straight line

zh-tw.content:
好起來這件事，不是一條直線。
有時候往前兩步，又退回來一步，那不是退步，那就是真實的康復。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種因為「明明上週還好好的，怎麼又掉回來」而責怪自己的人。療癒會反覆，倒退也是過程的一部分。適合在覺得自己又回到原點、很沮喪的時候讀。
variations: 退回來一點不代表前面都白走了，你還是在往前的路上。
情緒會起伏，這不是你做錯了什麼。

en.content:
Getting better isn't a straight line.
Sometimes you move two steps forward and slip one step back — that's not regression, that's what real recovery looks like.
en.editorNote: For people who blame themselves when they 'were fine last week and somehow slid back.' Healing loops; the backslides are part of it. Good to read when you feel like you've returned to square one and it's crushing you.
variations: Slipping back a little doesn't undo the distance you covered. You're still on the way forward.
Your mood will rise and fall. That doesn't mean you did something wrong.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by innertune.com 'emotions are messengers' theme; rewritten on feelings as messengers rather than problems

zh-tw.content:
情緒不是要被消滅的麻煩，
它們比較像來敲門的訊息：嘿，有些事你還沒好好看見。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一有負面情緒就急著趕走、覺得自己「不該這樣想」的人。情緒在告訴你一些事，先聽聽看，再決定要不要照做。適合在被某種說不上來的煩躁卡住的時候讀。
variations: 與其問「我幹嘛又這樣」，不如問「它想告訴我什麼」。
壞情緒不是敵人，是還沒被聽懂的自己。

en.content:
Emotions aren't problems to be eliminated.
They're more like messengers knocking at the door: hey, there's something you haven't really looked at yet.
en.editorNote: For people who rush to chase away every negative feeling and tell themselves they 'shouldn't think this way.' Emotions are pointing at something — listen first, then decide what to do. Good to read when you're stuck in a vague irritation you can't name.
variations: Instead of 'why am I like this again,' try asking 'what is this trying to tell me.'
A bad mood isn't the enemy. It's a part of you that hasn't been understood yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by blurtitout.org 'allowed to begin again' theme; rewritten on permission to start over without a quota

zh-tw.content:
你可以重新開始，
想重來幾次就重來幾次，沒有人在數你用掉了多少次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我已經試太多次了，再失敗就太丟臉」的人。重來沒有上限，也沒有人在旁邊計分。適合在又想放棄、卻又捨不得放棄的拉扯時刻讀。
variations: 失敗不會用完你重來的額度，因為根本沒有額度這回事。
今天又重新開始一次，這件事本身就值得肯定。

en.content:
You're allowed to begin again —
as many times as you need. No one is keeping count of how many you've used up.
en.editorNote: For people who feel like 'I've already tried too many times, failing again would be humiliating.' There's no cap on starting over, and no one's keeping score on the sidelines. Good to read in that tug-of-war when you want to quit but can't quite bring yourself to.
variations: Failing doesn't burn through your do-overs, because there's no such thing as a limit on them.
You started over again today. That alone is worth something.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by univself.com '用最溫柔的方式把自己打醒' theme; rewritten on gentleness softening the world's sharp edges

zh-tw.content:
當你願意對自己溫柔一點，
這個世界尖銳的地方，好像也跟著變鈍了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種習慣用嚴厲鞭策自己、覺得溫柔等於鬆懈的人。你怎麼對自己，會決定你怎麼感受整個世界。適合在被一整天的壓力磨得很硬的晚上讀。
variations: 對自己兇，世界就跟著兇；對自己軟一點，日子也會跟著鬆一點。
溫柔不是軟弱，是你選擇先善待自己。

en.content:
The moment you choose to be a little gentler with yourself,
the sharp edges of the world somehow start to feel duller too.
en.editorNote: For people who drive themselves with harshness and equate gentleness with slacking off. How you treat yourself shapes how the whole world feels to you. Good to read on a night when a full day of pressure has left you feeling hard and brittle.
variations: Be harsh with yourself and the world turns harsh too. Soften toward yourself, and the days loosen up a little.
Gentleness isn't weakness. It's choosing to treat yourself well first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by manmandays.com 放下感情語錄 theme; rewritten on letting go being a choice to love yourself, not an admission of defeat

zh-tw.content:
放下不代表你輸了。
只是你終於決定，要把多出來的力氣，留給更值得的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些把「放手」當成失敗、覺得撐住才算贏的人。放下是一種把資源重新分配給自己的選擇。適合在一段關係或一件事終於要結束時讀。
variations: 你不是放棄了，你只是不想再為了不對的東西，把自己耗光。
鬆開手不是認輸，是你決定不再讓它佔用你的人生。

en.content:
Letting go doesn't mean you lost.
It means you finally decided to save the leftover energy for a version of you that deserves it more.
en.editorNote: For people who treat letting go as failure and think holding on is the only way to win. Releasing something is a choice to reallocate your resources back to yourself. Good to read when a relationship or chapter is finally coming to an end.
variations: You didn't give up. You just stopped letting the wrong thing drain you dry.
Loosening your grip isn't surrender. It's deciding to stop letting it take up your life.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by mindfulnessexercises.com healing quotes (self-compassion theme); rewritten on giving yourself the kindness you'd give a friend doing their best

zh-tw.content:
對自己溫柔一點吧，
你其實已經在用現在能有的力氣，把日子盡量過好了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得自己「明明可以更好」而一直苛責自己的人。你不是不夠努力，你只是已經很累了。適合在被自我批判的聲音淹沒的時候讀。
variations: 你在用你現在僅有的方式撐著，這本身就已經很不容易了。
別再罵那個已經盡力的自己了，他比誰都需要你的體諒。

en.content:
Be a little gentler with yourself.
You're already getting through your days with whatever strength you happen to have right now.
en.editorNote: For people who keep scolding themselves with 'I should be doing better.' It's not that you're not trying — you're just tired. Good to read when the self-critical voice is drowning everything else out.
variations: You're holding it together the only way you can right now, and that alone is no small thing.
Stop yelling at the version of you who's already doing their best. They need your patience more than anyone.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by deliberatedirections.com healing & recovery quotes (Akshay Dubey theme on healing not erasing damage); rewritten on damage no longer steering your life

zh-tw.content:
療癒不是讓那段傷從來沒發生過，
而是有一天，它不再決定你今天要怎麼活。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些以為「好起來」就等於要徹底忘記、徹底沒事的人。傷可以還在，只是不再握著你的方向盤。適合在覺得自己「為什麼還沒完全好」而焦慮的時候讀。
variations: 你不用假裝它沒發生，你只需要讓它不再主導你的每一天。
痊癒不是回到受傷前，而是帶著它，依然走得動。

en.content:
Healing isn't pretending the hurt never happened.
It's the day it stops deciding how you get to live.
en.editorNote: For people who think 'getting better' means forgetting completely and being totally fine. The hurt can still be there — it just isn't holding your steering wheel anymore. Good to read when you're anxious about 'why am I still not fully over it.'
variations: You don't have to pretend it didn't happen. You just have to stop letting it run your days.
Healing isn't going back to before the wound. It's carrying it and still being able to walk.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by vocus.cc 療癒幸福語錄 theme '接納世界的不完美，你將更輕鬆'; rewritten on accepting imperfection as the thing that finally lets you breathe

zh-tw.content:
當你願意接受事情不會永遠剛好、人不會永遠如你所願，
你會發現，肩膀突然鬆了下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些一直想把所有事情都掌控到位、結果累到喘不過氣的人。接受不完美不是妥協，是放自己一馬。適合在因為「事情沒照計畫走」而煩躁的時候讀。
variations: 不是世界要變完美你才能放鬆，是你先願意鬆手，日子才開始輕。
接受有些事就是不會剛好，你反而能好好喘口氣了。

en.content:
The moment you accept that things won't always line up and people won't always do what you hoped,
you'll notice your shoulders quietly drop.
en.editorNote: For people who try to control every detail into place and end up too exhausted to breathe. Accepting imperfection isn't settling — it's cutting yourself some slack. Good to read when you're frustrated because things didn't go to plan.
variations: The world doesn't have to become perfect for you to relax. You loosen your grip first, and then the days get lighter.
Accept that some things just won't line up, and suddenly you can actually catch your breath.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by cmykhuang.com 守護天使療癒語錄 theme '當你對自己溫柔，世界的尖銳也會變得柔軟'; rewritten on how gentleness toward yourself softens everything else

zh-tw.content:
當你開始對自己溫柔一點，
你會發現，連這個世界的稜角，看起來都沒那麼尖了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些對自己很兇、覺得全世界都在跟自己作對的人。很多時候不是世界變了，是你看自己的眼神先變柔軟了。適合在一整天都覺得被刁難之後，睡前讀給自己聽。
variations: 你怎麼對自己，往往就決定了你怎麼感受這個世界。
先對自己鬆一口氣，外面的事才會跟著好相處一點。

en.content:
The moment you start being a little gentler with yourself,
you'll notice even the world's sharp edges don't look so sharp anymore.
en.editorNote: For people who are hard on themselves and feel like the whole world is against them. Often it isn't the world that changes — it's the way you look at yourself softening first. Good to read before bed after a day that felt like one slight after another.
variations: How you treat yourself tends to decide how the whole world feels to you.
Cut yourself some slack first, and everything out there gets easier to deal with.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Healgood Counseling 2026 gentle reminders 'Healing is rarely linear. You are allowed to begin again, as many times as needed'; rewritten on permission to restart without shame

zh-tw.content:
好起來這條路不是直的。
你可以倒退、可以原地停一下，也可以重來，要幾次都行。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些以為「復原應該越來越好、不該退步」的人。退一步不代表前功盡棄，療癒本來就會反覆。適合在覺得自己「又掉回去了」、開始自責的時候讀。
variations: 退步不是失敗，是復原本來就長這個樣子。
你被允許重新開始，不限次數，也不用解釋。

en.content:
The road to feeling better isn't a straight line.
You're allowed to slide back, to pause where you are, to start over — as many times as you need.
en.editorNote: For people who think recovery should only ever improve and never regress. A step back doesn't erase your progress — healing was always going to loop. Good to read when you feel like you've 'slipped backward' and the self-blame is kicking in.
variations: A setback isn't failure. It's just what recovery actually looks like.
You're allowed to begin again — no limit on how many times, no explanation owed.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paige Rechtman LMHC self-compassion theme 'Be gentle with yourself; you're doing the best you can'; rewritten on the version of you that's quietly trying its hardest

zh-tw.content:
你已經在用你現在有的力氣，做到你能做的了。
別再回頭罵那個正在拼命的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些事情沒做好就狠狠檢討自己的人。你當下的盡力，是用當下的狀態去衡量的，不是用別人的標準。適合在搞砸了某件事、開始自我攻擊的那一刻讀。
variations: 用現在的條件做到現在的程度，已經很不容易了。
那個咬牙撐著的自己，值得的是一句辛苦了，不是責備。

en.content:
With the energy you have right now, you're already doing what you can.
Stop turning around to scold the version of you that's trying so hard.
en.editorNote: For people who interrogate themselves the moment something doesn't go well. Your best is measured against the state you're actually in — not against someone else's standard. Good to read in the moment you've messed something up and the self-attack is starting.
variations: Doing this much with the conditions you've got is already a lot.
The version of you white-knuckling through this deserves a 'that was hard,' not a lecture.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by vocus.cc 療癒幸福語錄 theme '在喧囂裡給自己留一片安靜，聆聽內心的聲音'; rewritten on carving out a small pocket of quiet that belongs only to you

zh-tw.content:
再忙的一天，也留五分鐘給自己。
不滑手機、不想待辦事項，只是安靜地坐著，聽聽自己現在好不好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些一整天被訊息和待辦事項追著跑、連喘口氣都覺得奢侈的人。獨處不是浪費時間，是把自己找回來。適合在感覺整個人都不是自己的時候，刻意停下來做。
variations: 你不需要一整段假期才能休息，五分鐘的安靜也算數。
在最吵的日子裡，替自己留一個只屬於你的安靜角落。

en.content:
However busy the day gets, save five minutes for yourself.
No scrolling, no running through the to-do list — just sit quietly and check how you're actually doing.
en.editorNote: For people chased all day by messages and tasks, who feel like even a breath is a luxury. Solitude isn't wasted time — it's how you find yourself again. Good to do deliberately on days when you feel like you're not even yourself anymore.
variations: You don't need a whole vacation to rest. Five minutes of quiet counts too.
On the loudest days, keep one quiet corner that belongs only to you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Mental Health Center Kids 2026 quotes theme 'You are enough, just as you are'; rewritten on worthiness that doesn't need to be earned or upgraded

zh-tw.content:
你不是修好了哪裡、補上了什麼才值得被愛。
你現在這個樣子，原原本本的，就已經足夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些總覺得「等我變得更好，才配得到好東西」的人。你的值得不是有條件的，不需要先升級再來領。適合在覺得自己「還不夠好」而退縮的時候讀給自己。
variations: 你不需要先變成更好的版本，才有資格對自己好。
你本來就夠了，這句話不附帶任何條件。

en.content:
You don't become worthy of love by fixing some flaw or adding some missing piece.
The way you are right now, exactly as is, is already enough.
en.editorNote: For people who keep thinking 'once I'm better, then I'll deserve good things.' Your worth isn't conditional — there's no upgrade to complete before you can collect it. Good to read when you're holding back because you feel 'not good enough yet.'
variations: You don't have to become a better version of yourself first to deserve kindness.
You're already enough — and that comes with no conditions attached.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff / self-compassion theme 'give yourself the kindness you'd give a friend'; rewritten on talking to yourself the way you'd talk to someone you love

zh-tw.content:
如果你最好的朋友把你心裡那些話對自己說，你一定會心疼。
那就別再那樣對自己說話了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給習慣在心裡狠狠罵自己的人。你對朋友從來不會那麼苛刻，自己也值得同樣的溫柔。適合在自我檢討到快受不了的時候，停下來提醒自己一次。
variations: 對自己講話的口氣，試著像對一個你很在乎的人那樣。
你不會這樣罵你愛的人，那就也別這樣罵自己。

en.content:
If your best friend talked to themselves the way you talk to yourself, it would break your heart.
So stop talking to yourself that way.
en.editorNote: For people who beat themselves up in their own head. You'd never be that harsh with a friend — you deserve the same gentleness. Good to pause on when the self-criticism gets to be too much.
variations: Try speaking to yourself the way you'd speak to someone you care about.
You wouldn't say it to someone you love. So don't say it to yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 《療癒，從感受情緒開始》theme '情緒沒有對錯，傳遞內在需求的訊息'; rewritten on emotions being messengers, not problems

zh-tw.content:
情緒沒有好壞，它只是來告訴你，你心裡少了什麼、又需要什麼。
你不用趕它走，聽它說完就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「不該有負面情緒」而拼命壓抑的人。難過、生氣、焦慮都不是錯，它們是訊息。適合在情緒上來、卻又怪自己不該這樣的時候讀。
variations: 你不用消滅情緒，只要願意聽它想說什麼。
情緒不是來找你麻煩的，是來告訴你需要被照顧了。

en.content:
Emotions aren't good or bad. They just come to tell you what you're missing and what you need.
You don't have to chase them away — just let them finish talking.
en.editorNote: For people who think they 'shouldn't' have negative feelings and bottle everything up. Sadness, anger, anxiety aren't mistakes — they're messages. Good to read when a feeling rises and you start blaming yourself for having it.
variations: You don't have to get rid of a feeling — just be willing to hear it out.
Your emotions aren't here to give you trouble. They're here to say you need some care.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by The Nap Ministry theme 'rest is not weakness, it's nourishment'; rewritten on rest as something you don't have to earn

zh-tw.content:
休息不是做完所有事才能拿到的獎勵，
它本來就是你的，不用先累到不行才准許自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「還沒做完就不配休息」的人。休息是基本需求，不是表現好才發的獎品。適合在硬撐、卻又愧疚自己想停下來的時候。
variations: 你不需要先把自己榨乾，才有資格喘一口氣。
想休息不需要理由，累本身就是理由。

en.content:
Rest isn't a prize you collect after finishing everything.
It was always yours — you don't have to run yourself into the ground before you're allowed it.
en.editorNote: For people who feel they 'haven't earned' a break yet. Rest is a basic need, not a reward for good performance. Good for when you're pushing through but feeling guilty for wanting to stop.
variations: You don't have to drain yourself dry before you're allowed to catch your breath.
You don't need a reason to rest. Being tired is reason enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 mental health quotes theme on comparison and personal timing; rewritten on running your own race at your own pace

zh-tw.content:
別人走得快，不代表你走得慢。
你們本來就不在同一條路上，沒什麼好比的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些看著別人前進、就開始焦慮自己落後的人。每個人的時區不一樣，你的步調沒有錯。適合在被「同齡人都已經…」這種念頭追著跑的時候。
variations: 你不是慢，你只是在走自己的路。
花有花期，你也有你的，急不來。

en.content:
Just because someone else is moving fast doesn't mean you're moving slow.
You were never on the same road — there's nothing to compare.
en.editorNote: For people who watch others get ahead and start panicking that they're falling behind. Everyone's on their own clock; your pace isn't wrong. Good for when the 'everyone my age has already…' thoughts are chasing you.
variations: You're not slow — you're just walking your own road.
Flowers bloom on their own schedule. So do you. It can't be rushed.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Adler / 接納自己的不完美 theme '療癒從接納不完美的自己開始'; rewritten on imperfection being what makes you human

zh-tw.content:
你不用變得完美才值得被愛。
那些你想藏起來的不完美，剛好就是讓你像個人、惹人疼的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「要先修好自己」才敢靠近別人的人。完美沒有人味，缺口才有溫度。適合在因為某個缺點而否定整個自己的時候，讀給自己聽。
variations: 缺一角才裝得進別人的光，完整反而沒有縫隙。
你不是要把不完美藏好，而是學會帶著它一起被愛。

en.content:
You don't have to become perfect to be worthy of love.
The flaws you want to hide are exactly what make you human — and easy to love.
en.editorNote: For people who feel they have to 'fix themselves' before letting anyone close. Perfection has no warmth; it's the cracks that do. Good to read when one flaw makes you write off your whole self.
variations: It's the missing piece that lets someone else's light in — wholeness leaves no gap.
You don't have to hide your imperfections. You learn to be loved with them.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'rest is not laziness, it is a need' theme; rewritten on rest being a biological need, not a moral failing

zh-tw.content:
你不是因為需要休息就變成了壞人。
累了想停下來，是身體在說話，不是你不夠努力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些一休息就有罪惡感的人。休息不是獎勵，是必需品。適合在硬撐到快垮掉、卻還在罵自己懶的時候讀給自己聽。
variations: 休息不用先賺到，它本來就是你的權利。
停下來不代表你輸了，只代表你終於聽見自己了。

en.content:
Needing rest doesn't make you a bad person.
When you're tired and want to stop, that's your body talking — not proof that you slacked off.
en.editorNote: For people who feel guilty the moment they rest. Rest isn't a reward; it's a requirement. Read this when you're running on empty and still calling yourself lazy.
variations: You don't have to earn rest. It was always yours.
Stopping doesn't mean you lost. It means you finally heard yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing doesn't mean the damage never existed' theme; rewritten on healing as the wound losing its grip, not disappearing

zh-tw.content:
痊癒不是當作那件事沒發生過，
而是那道傷，再也決定不了你要往哪裡走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給以為「還會想起就代表沒好」的人。傷痕留著沒關係，它只是過去，不再是方向盤。適合在被舊事突然刺到、又懷疑自己白走一遭的時候。
variations: 你不用忘記，只要它不再牽著你的鼻子走就好。
好起來不是傷不見了，是你終於能繞過它走路。

en.content:
Healing isn't pretending it never happened.
It's the wound losing the power to decide where you go next.
en.editorNote: For people who think 'if it still hurts, I'm not healed.' The scar can stay — it's just the past, not the steering wheel. Read this when an old memory blindsides you and you wonder if you made any progress at all.
variations: You don't have to forget. You just have to stop letting it lead you by the nose.
Getting better isn't the wound vanishing — it's you finally being able to walk around it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing doesn't follow a straight line, setbacks are valid' theme; rewritten on recovery being non-linear

zh-tw.content:
好起來這條路不是直線。
今天比昨天退了一點，也是路上的一部分，不是退步，是你還在走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些以為「狀態變好就該一路往上」的人。情緒會反覆很正常，跌回去不等於前面都白費。適合在以為自己好了、卻又突然低落而自責的時候。
variations: 進兩步退一步，整體還是在往前，這也算數。
復原沒有進度條，難過的那天也是復原的一天。

en.content:
Getting better isn't a straight line.
A day that feels worse than yesterday is still part of the road — not a relapse, just proof you're still walking it.
en.editorNote: For people who expect recovery to only go up. Feelings loop back, and slipping doesn't erase the progress before it. Read this when you thought you were fine and suddenly crashed, then blamed yourself for it.
variations: Two steps forward, one step back still counts as forward.
Recovery has no progress bar. The bad days are recovery days too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'treat yourself with the kindness you'd offer your dearest friend' theme; rewritten on turning the inner critic into a friend

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友那麼溫柔，怎麼對自己這麼兇。
下次想罵自己之前，先問：如果是朋友，我會這樣說他嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些對別人很寬容、對自己卻很苛刻的人。把那套溫柔分一點給自己，不是縱容，是公平。適合在搞砸一件事、開始狠狠檢討自己的時候。
variations: 你值得被自己善待，就像你一直善待別人那樣。
腦子裡那個一直挑剔你的聲音，不一定是對的，也不一定是你。

en.content:
You're so gentle with your friends — why so harsh with yourself?
Next time you're about to tear yourself apart, ask: would I ever say this to a friend?
en.editorNote: For people who are generous with everyone but brutal with themselves. Lending yourself some of that kindness isn't going soft — it's just being fair. Read this when you mess something up and start ripping into yourself.
variations: You deserve the same kindness from yourself that you've always given others.
That nitpicking voice in your head isn't always right — and it isn't always you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing is not linear, good days and bad days are both okay' theme; rewritten on non-linear recovery

zh-tw.content:
復原從來不是一條直線。
今天好一點、明天又退回去，都不代表你失敗，只代表你還在路上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些以為「好起來」就該一天比一天好的人。退步不是歸零，是過程的一部分。適合在覺得自己又被打回原形、開始懷疑努力沒用的時候看。
variations: 你不是又倒退了，你只是在一條本來就會起起伏伏的路上走著。
有好日子也有壞日子，這不叫沒進步，這叫真實的康復。

en.content:
Healing was never a straight line.
Better today, worse tomorrow — that's not failure. It just means you're still on the way.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks getting better should mean every day is brighter than the last. A setback isn't a reset — it's part of the process. Read this when you feel like you're back at square one and start doubting it was worth it.
variations: You didn't slide backward. You're just walking a road that was always going to rise and dip.
Good days and bad days both — that's not a lack of progress, that's what real recovery looks like.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing is letting go of everything that isn't you' theme; rewritten on shedding others' expectations

zh-tw.content:
也許療癒不是變成更好的人，
而是慢慢放掉那些本來就不屬於你的期待，回到原本的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些活得很用力、卻越來越不像自己的人。療癒有時候不是加法，是減法。適合在發現自己一直在滿足別人、卻忘了自己想要什麼的時候。
variations: 你不需要變成誰，你只需要把別人塞給你的那些卸下來。
回到自己，不是退步，是把走遠的路慢慢收回來。

en.content:
Maybe healing isn't about becoming a better person.
Maybe it's slowly letting go of everything that was never yours — and coming back to who you already are.
en.editorNote: For people living hard but feeling less and less like themselves. Sometimes healing isn't addition, it's subtraction. Read this when you realize you've been busy meeting everyone's expectations and forgot what you wanted.
variations: You don't have to become someone. You just have to set down what others handed you.
Returning to yourself isn't going backward — it's quietly walking back the road you wandered too far down.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'flowers don't worry about how they bloom, they just turn toward the light' theme; rewritten on trusting your own pace

zh-tw.content:
花從來不擔心自己要怎麼開，
它只是朝著光，然後在該開的時候開了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些一直焦慮「我是不是太慢了」的人。有些事不是用力就會快，是時間到了自然會發生。適合在跟別人比較、覺得自己落後的時候。
variations: 你不用知道全部的答案，朝著讓你舒服一點的方向走就好。
該你的時間還沒到而已，不是你不夠好。

en.content:
A flower never worries about how it's supposed to bloom.
It just turns toward the light — and opens when it's time.
en.editorNote: For anyone stuck on 'am I falling behind?' Some things don't speed up no matter how hard you push — they happen when the time comes. Read this when you're comparing yourself to others and feeling slow.
variations: You don't need all the answers. Just keep turning toward whatever makes you breathe a little easier.
Your time just hasn't come yet — that's not the same as you not being enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'be gentle with yourself, you are doing the best you can' theme; rewritten on self-permission to be imperfect

zh-tw.content:
對自己溫柔一點，
你已經在自己能力範圍內，盡力了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得自己「明明可以更好」而一直自責的人。盡力了就值得被肯定，不是只有結果完美才算數。適合在一天結束、覺得自己什麼都沒做好的夜裡。
variations: 你不是不夠努力，你只是太習慣看自己沒做到的部分。
今天能撐過來，就已經很了不起了。

en.content:
Be a little gentler with yourself.
Within everything you had to work with, you've already done your best.
en.editorNote: For people who beat themselves up over 'I could've done better.' Doing your best is worth acknowledging — the result doesn't have to be perfect to count. Read this at the end of a day when you feel like you got nothing right.
variations: You're not lazy — you're just used to staring only at the parts you didn't finish.
You made it through today. That alone is already a lot.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by PopLady 溫柔語錄 collection theme on letting emotions exist; rewritten on allowing feelings without rushing to fix them

zh-tw.content:
每一種心情都有它存在的理由，
別急著消化它們，先讓自己好好感覺一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些難過時第一反應是「我不該這樣」的人。情緒不是要你立刻處理掉的問題，先承認它在，反而會輕一點。適合在心裡悶悶的、又說不上來為什麼的時候。
variations: 你可以難過，這不需要理由，也不需要趕快好起來。
情緒不是來搗亂的，它只是想告訴你一些事，聽一下就好。

en.content:
Every feeling has its reason for being here.
Don't rush to process them away — let yourself actually feel it first.
en.editorNote: For people whose first reaction to sadness is 'I shouldn't feel this way.' An emotion isn't a problem to fix immediately — just admitting it's there usually makes it lighter. Read this when something feels heavy and you can't quite say why.
variations: You're allowed to be sad. It doesn't need a reason, and it doesn't need to hurry up and pass.
Your feelings aren't here to wreck things — they're just trying to tell you something. Listen for a second.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion theme: 'your pace is valid; healing does not respond to pressure'; rewritten with everyday Taiwan phrasing

zh-tw.content:
你的速度沒有錯。
復原這件事，不會因為你催它就走得快一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合一直覺得自己「好得太慢」而自責的人。把復原想成發芽，你再急，它也只會按自己的節奏長。傳給最近覺得卡住、進度落後的朋友。
variations: 別人走得快是別人的事，你按你的節奏走，一樣會到。
好起來沒有時間表，你不是落後，你只是在自己的時區裡。

en.content:
Your pace isn't wrong.
Healing doesn't move faster just because you rush it.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps blaming themselves for 'healing too slowly.' Think of recovery like a seed sprouting — no matter how much you hurry it, it grows on its own clock. Send to a friend who feels stuck or behind.
variations: Other people moving fast is their business. Go at your own speed — you'll still get there.
Getting better doesn't run on a schedule. You're not behind; you're just on your own timezone.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'some wounds heal quietly, and that's still healing'; rewritten as gentle permission-giving line

zh-tw.content:
有些傷是默默好起來的，
沒有人看見，不代表它沒在好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「明明很努力卻好像沒進展」的低潮時刻。很多復原是看不見的，就像骨頭在石膏裡接合，外面靜悄悄，裡面一直在動。提醒自己安靜不等於停滯。
variations: 你正在好起來，只是這次它選擇用很安靜的方式。
看起來沒變化，不代表裡面什麼都沒發生。

en.content:
Some wounds heal quietly.
No one seeing it doesn't mean it isn't happening.
en.editorNote: For the slump where you feel like you're trying hard but going nowhere. So much recovery is invisible — like a bone knitting back together inside a cast: silent on the outside, working the whole time. A reminder that quiet isn't the same as stuck.
variations: You're healing — it just chose to do it quietly this time.
Looking unchanged on the outside doesn't mean nothing's happening inside.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'give yourself the softness you offer everyone else'; rewritten toward self-kindness usage

zh-tw.content:
你對別人那麼溫柔，
記得也留一點那種溫柔給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給總是先照顧別人、最後才輪到自己的那種人。你勸朋友的那些話「沒關係、你已經很棒了」，自己也值得聽一次。當你又開始對自己很嚴格時，看一下。
variations: 你安慰別人時那麼會講話，也用同一張嘴對自己好一點。
你願意原諒朋友的失誤，那也試著原諒一次自己。

en.content:
You're so gentle with everyone else —
save a little of that gentleness for yourself too.
en.editorNote: For the person who always takes care of others first and themselves last. The things you tell a friend — 'it's okay, you're already doing great' — you deserve to hear them once too. Read this when you catch yourself being harsh on yourself again.
variations: You're so kind when you comfort other people. Use that same voice on yourself.
You forgive your friends' mistakes easily. Try forgiving yourself one too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '允許自己慢慢前行，不必追趕世界的節奏'; rewritten as rest-permission line

zh-tw.content:
累了就停下來，不用先有什麼成績才配休息。
你撐到現在，本身就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種覺得「沒做完事就不敢休息」的人。休息不是獎賞，是必需品。提醒一直緊繃的自己或朋友，喘口氣不需要理由。
variations: 休息不用先賺到，你想停下來的時候就可以停。
你不是機器，沒有非得運轉到壞掉不可。

en.content:
If you're tired, stop. You don't need to earn rest with results first.
Making it this far is already enough.
en.editorNote: For anyone who 'doesn't dare rest until everything's done.' Rest isn't a reward — it's a necessity. A reminder for the constantly-tense you (or a friend) that catching your breath needs no excuse.
variations: You don't have to earn rest. You can stop whenever you want to.
You're not a machine. You don't have to run until you break.
