items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are allowed to be both a masterpiece and a work in progress simultaneously'; rewritten in casual Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
你可以同時是「已經很好」跟「還在變更好」。
這兩件事從來不衝突。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種覺得「還沒完美就不能喜歡自己」的人。你不必等到變成最終版本才值得被善待，半成品也是有人愛的。卡在自我懷疑時讀一下。
variations: 你不用等到完美才開始喜歡自己，現在這樣就可以喜歡了。
還在進步，跟已經夠好，可以同時成立。

en.content:
You can be 'already good' and 'still becoming better' at the same time.
Those two were never in conflict.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I can't like myself until I'm perfect.' You don't have to wait for the final version to deserve kindness — the work-in-progress is lovable too. Read this when self-doubt has you stuck.
variations: You don't have to wait until you're perfect to start liking yourself. You can like yourself as you are now.
Still growing and already enough can both be true at once.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion theme 'rest is regulation, not procrastination'; rewritten in casual Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
休息不是偷懶，是你的身體在幫你踩煞車。
它比你更早知道你撐不住了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種一停下來就有罪惡感的人。把休息當成放縱的話，會永遠覺得自己不夠努力。傳給最近狂工作、連睡覺都覺得對不起誰的朋友。
variations: 你不是懶，你只是太久沒讓自己停下來。
想休息不需要理由，累了就是最好的理由。

en.content:
Rest isn't slacking — it's your body hitting the brakes for you.
It knew you were running on empty before you did.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels guilty the moment they stop. If you treat rest as indulgence, you'll always feel like you haven't done enough. Send to a friend who's been grinding and feels bad even about sleeping.
variations: You're not lazy. You've just gone too long without letting yourself stop.
You don't need a reason to rest. Being tired is reason enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'anxiety is a sign your nervous system is working overtime to protect you'; rewritten with gentle, non-clinical phrasing

zh-tw.content:
你的焦慮不是缺陷，是你的身體太認真在保護你了。
它只是有點用力過頭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合因為「又焦慮了」而責怪自己的人。焦慮不代表你壞掉，它其實是你太在乎、太想做好。讀完可以對自己溫柔一點，謝謝那個一直警戒的自己。
variations: 會焦慮不是你不夠堅強，是你的身體把保護你這件事做得太徹底了。
別怪那個一直緊張的自己，他只是太怕你受傷。

en.content:
Your anxiety isn't a flaw — it's your body taking 'protect you' way too seriously.
It just got a little overzealous.
en.editorNote: For anyone who beats themselves up for 'being anxious again.' Anxiety doesn't mean you're broken — it usually means you care, and you want to get it right. Read it, then go easy on the part of you that's always on guard.
variations: Being anxious doesn't make you weak. Your body just took protecting you all the way.
Don't blame the part of you that keeps tensing up. It's just terrified of you getting hurt.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by healing-transformation idea of dropping 'I shouldn't feel this way' and just accompanying oneself; rewritten in conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
你不必先把心情整理好才允許自己難過。
難過的時候，陪著自己就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種一有負面情緒就急著「我不應該這樣」的人。情緒不需要先被批准才能存在。下次難過時，先別急著趕走它，坐在它旁邊一下下也沒關係。
variations: 不用等到想通了才能哭，先哭也可以。
情緒來了就讓它在，你不用馬上把它處理掉。

en.content:
You don't have to tidy up your feelings before you're allowed to be sad.
When it hurts, just keeping yourself company is enough.
en.editorNote: For the person who rushes to 'I shouldn't feel this way' the second a hard emotion shows up. Feelings don't need approval to exist. Next time it hurts, don't shoo it away — sitting beside it for a bit is okay too.
variations: You don't have to figure it all out before you cry. Crying first is allowed.
When a feeling shows up, let it stay. You don't have to deal with it right away.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'other people's evaluation is no longer the standard of your existence'; rewritten in warm Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
別人怎麼看你，是他們的功課，不是你的。
你不用活成每個人都滿意的版本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種太在意別人眼光、活得很累的人。你不可能讓所有人都喜歡你，光是這件事就會耗掉你一輩子。把力氣留給真正在乎你的人就好。
variations: 你不是來通過每個人的審核的，你是來好好活的。
讓所有人都滿意是不可能的任務，放過自己吧。

en.content:
What people think of you is their homework, not yours.
You don't have to become the version everyone approves of.
en.editorNote: For anyone worn out from caring too much about how others see them. You can't get everyone to like you — chasing that alone could eat up a whole lifetime. Save your energy for the people who actually care about you.
variations: You're not here to pass everyone's inspection. You're here to live.
Pleasing everyone is an impossible job. Let yourself off the hook.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by recovery theme of self-awareness leading to self-acceptance and walking out of a low point; rewritten gently

zh-tw.content:
走出低谷不是一步登天，是一天比一天少難過一點點。
你正在好起來，只是你自己沒發現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合正在低潮、覺得自己永遠好不了的人。康復從來不是一夕之間，而是慢慢的、幾乎察覺不到的進步。回頭看看，你已經比最糟的時候走遠了。
variations: 好起來不會敲鑼打鼓，它是悄悄發生的。
你不用一次就走出來，每天少痛一點也算數。

en.content:
Climbing out of a low point isn't one giant leap — it's hurting a little less each day.
You're already getting better. You just haven't noticed yet.
en.editorNote: For anyone in a slump who feels like they'll never be okay again. Recovery is never overnight — it's slow, almost imperceptible progress. Look back: you've already come further than your worst day.
variations: Getting better doesn't arrive with fanfare. It happens quietly.
You don't have to climb out all at once. Hurting a little less each day counts too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by non-linear healing theme: some days strong, some days heavy, both valid; rewritten in own words

zh-tw.content:
好起來不是一條直線。
有些日子你會覺得有力氣，有些日子又重得抬不起頭。
兩種都算數，不用因為退步就否定自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給狀態忽好忽壞、覺得自己「明明前幾天還好好的」而自責的人。提醒對方復原本來就會起起伏伏，倒退一步不代表前面的努力白費。
variations: 今天不好沒關係，不代表你又回到原點了。
進步不是看你走多快，是看你有沒有對自己誠實。

en.content:
Getting better isn't a straight line.
Some days you'll feel strong. Some days you'll be too heavy to lift your head.
Both count. A bad day doesn't erase the good ones.
en.editorNote: For someone whose mood swings up and down and who beats themselves up because they 'were fine just a few days ago.' Reminds them recovery is supposed to wobble — one step back doesn't cancel the progress.
variations: Having a rough day doesn't mean you're back at square one.
Progress isn't about how fast you go — it's about being honest with yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are not weak for needing time to heal, you are human'; rewritten gently

zh-tw.content:
需要時間才能好起來，不是你太脆弱，
只是你也是人而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「別人都撐得住，為什麼只有我這麼弱」的人。把需要時間療傷講成一件再正常不過的事，幫對方卸下那份多餘的羞愧感。
variations: 會痛、會需要慢慢復原，這很正常，你沒有壞掉。
別人的進度不是你的標準，你按自己的時間來就好。

en.content:
Needing time to heal doesn't make you weak.
It just makes you human.
en.editorNote: For anyone thinking 'everyone else copes, why am I the only one who can't.' Reframes needing time as completely ordinary, lifting off the extra shame they're carrying.
variations: Hurting and needing to mend slowly is normal. You're not broken.
Other people's pace isn't your benchmark. Go at your own.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '愛自己是一生的課題，別對自己太嚴格'; original rewrite on being gentle with oneself

zh-tw.content:
你對別人那麼溫柔，記得也留一點給自己。
當你對自己溫柔，世界的尖銳也會跟著變鈍。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種對朋友超級體貼、卻對自己特別嚴格的人。提醒對方把平常照顧別人的那份溫柔，分一些回來給自己。
variations: 你願意原諒別人的失誤，也試著原諒一下自己吧。
對自己好不是自私，是你撐下去的方式。

en.content:
You're so gentle with everyone else — save a little of that for yourself.
When you're kind to you, the world's sharp edges get a little softer too.
en.editorNote: For the person who's endlessly thoughtful toward friends but brutally hard on themselves. Reminds them to redirect some of that everyday kindness back inward.
variations: You forgive everyone else's slip-ups — try forgiving your own.
Being good to yourself isn't selfish. It's how you keep going.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'rest is not laziness, it's maintenance'; rewritten as permission-giving healing line

zh-tw.content:
休息不是偷懶，是保養。
機器不停運轉都會壞，更何況是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一停下來就有罪惡感的人。用「保養」這個角度，讓對方理解休息不是浪費時間，而是讓自己能走更遠的必要步驟。
variations: 你不是機器，本來就不該二十四小時待命。
先把自己照顧好，事情才有人能做。

en.content:
Rest isn't laziness — it's maintenance.
Even machines break if they never stop. You're not exempt.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels guilty the moment they slow down. The 'maintenance' angle reframes rest as a necessary step for going further, not wasted time.
variations: You're not a machine. You were never meant to be on call 24/7.
Take care of yourself first — there's no one to do the work if you fall apart.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion theme (Germer/Brené Brown angle); rewritten as a turn-the-kindness-inward line, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友那麼溫柔，
換你自己的時候，也記得留一點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人安慰別人很拿手，輪到自己就只剩責備。這句話提醒：你給別人的那份體諒，自己也值得分一份。適合傳給那種對誰都好、就是對自己最兇的人。
variations: 你會原諒朋友的失誤，那為什麼不肯放過自己一次？
對自己說話時，語氣可以柔一點，反正也沒人在趕你。

en.content:
You're so gentle with your friends.
Save a little of that for yourself.
en.editorNote: A lot of people are great at comforting others but only know how to scold themselves. The reminder: the kindness you hand out so freely is yours to keep too. Send it to the person who's good to everyone except themselves.
variations: You'd forgive a friend for slipping up — so why won't you let yourself off the hook once?
Soften your tone when you talk to yourself. No one's rushing you anyway.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are not weak for needing time to heal… you are human' theme; rewritten as permission-giving line

zh-tw.content:
需要時間復原，不代表你脆弱。
那只是代表，你是個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那些覺得「怎麼還沒好」而焦慮的人。療癒沒有進度表，慢一點也不是壞掉。適合傳給最近在低潮、又怪自己恢復太慢的朋友。
variations: 傷口要結痂也需要時間，心也是一樣的。
沒有人規定你幾天內要好起來，那本來就不是考試。

en.content:
Taking time to recover doesn't mean you're fragile.
It just means you're human.
en.editorNote: For anyone anxious that they're 'still not over it.' Healing has no deadline, and going slow isn't being broken. Good to send to a friend in a low spot who keeps blaming themselves for recovering too slowly.
variations: A wound needs time to scab over. So does a heart.
Nobody set a due date for you to feel okay. It was never an exam.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '接納才是最好的溫柔' acceptance theme; rewritten as a gentle acceptance line

zh-tw.content:
接納，是你能給生命最溫柔的事。
不論是迎接誰的出現，還是學會放手讓誰離開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聚散離合誰都躲不掉，硬撐著對抗反而更痛。這句話把「接受」說成一種溫柔，而不是認輸。適合在送別、或人生有變動時拿出來看。
variations: 有些事不是要你想通，是要你願意放它過去。
來的好好珍惜，走的就好好祝福，這樣就夠了。

en.content:
Acceptance is the gentlest thing you can offer your own life —
whether it's welcoming someone in, or learning to let someone go.
en.editorNote: Meetings and partings come for everyone, and white-knuckling against them only hurts more. This reframes 'accepting' as tenderness, not surrender. Good for a goodbye, or any season of change.
variations: Some things don't need you to figure them out — just to let them pass.
Cherish the ones who stay; bless the ones who go. That's enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by step-by-step '再長的路慢慢走也能走完' theme; rewritten as a pace-yourself line

zh-tw.content:
再長的路，一步一步也走得完。
你只是走得慢，不是停在原地。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得自己進度落後、快撐不住的人。重點不是走多快，是還在往前。適合傳給正在熬一段漫長過程的朋友，考試、復健、轉職都算。
variations: 進度條跑得慢，也還是在跑，這就夠了。
今天比昨天前進一點點，就值得給自己一個讚。

en.content:
Even the longest road gets finished one step at a time.
You're just moving slowly — you're not standing still.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels behind and almost out of steam. It's not about speed, it's about still moving forward. Send to a friend grinding through something long — exams, rehab, a career switch, any of it.
variations: A slow progress bar is still a moving one. That counts.
A little further today than yesterday is worth a quiet pat on the back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing doesn't always look like progress, sometimes it looks like breathing, resting, softening' theme; rewritten for permission-giving tone

zh-tw.content:
有些日子，能好好呼吸、好好睡一覺，
就已經是很努力了。
那也算前進。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們很容易把「有產出」當成唯一的進步標準，但有些日子光是撐著沒倒下就用盡全力了。這句話想對那種覺得「今天什麼都沒做完、好廢」的人說：你不是停滯，你只是在補血。傳給最近很累、卻還在責怪自己不夠拚的朋友。
variations: 今天沒崩潰，已經是很厲害的成績了。
休息不是偷懶，是身體在跟你討一點還回去的力氣。

en.content:
Some days, just breathing well and getting some sleep
is already trying hard.
That counts as moving forward, too.
en.editorNote: We default to treating 'being productive' as the only valid kind of progress, but some days just not falling apart takes everything you've got. This is for the person thinking 'I got nothing done today, I'm so useless' — you're not stuck, you're refueling. Send it to a tired friend who's still beating themselves up for not grinding harder.
variations: Not falling apart today is already a solid result.
Resting isn't slacking — it's your body asking for some of the energy back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are enough, just as you are' self-worth theme; rewritten to remove the pressure of proving

zh-tw.content:
你不用變成更好的人才值得被喜歡。
現在這個樣子的你，就已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人習慣先把自己罵一頓，覺得要先「夠好」才配得到溫柔。但溫柔不是獎品，不需要先達標。這句話想對那種總是覺得自己還不夠的人說：你不欠世界一個更完美的版本。適合傳給很努力卻常常覺得自己不夠格的朋友。
variations: 你不是有條件才值得被愛，你本來就值得。
先放過自己一馬，你已經做得比你以為的還多。

en.content:
You don't have to become a better person to deserve to be liked.
The you that exists right now is already enough.
en.editorNote: A lot of people roast themselves first, convinced they have to be 'good enough' before they're allowed any kindness. But kindness isn't a prize you unlock by hitting a target. This is for the person who always feels like they're not quite there yet — you don't owe the world a more perfect version of yourself. Send it to a hard-working friend who keeps feeling like they fall short.
variations: You're not worthy of love only when you meet some condition — you always were.
Cut yourself some slack. You've done more than you give yourself credit for.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are not weak for needing time to heal, you are human' theme; rewritten to normalize needing help

zh-tw.content:
需要時間，不代表你很弱。
會累、會想哭、會想找人靠一下，
這些都只是因為你是人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們常把「自己撐住」當成一種美德，搞得開口求助好像是認輸。可是再堅強的人也有需要被接住的時候。這句話想拆掉那個「我不能麻煩別人」的緊箍咒。傳給那種總是照顧別人、卻不允許自己軟弱的朋友。
variations: 想休息一下、想靠著誰喘口氣，都不是弱，是正常。
你可以堅強，也可以偶爾不堅強，這兩個不衝突。

en.content:
Needing time doesn't make you weak.
Getting tired, wanting to cry, wanting someone to lean on —
that's just you being human.
en.editorNote: We treat 'toughing it out alone' as a virtue, which makes asking for help feel like admitting defeat. But even the strongest people need to be caught sometimes. This one's here to loosen that 'I can't be a burden' grip. Send it to the friend who takes care of everyone else but never lets themselves fall apart.
variations: Wanting to rest or lean on someone for a sec isn't weakness — it's normal.
You can be strong and also not-strong sometimes. The two don't cancel out.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing-drama line about coexisting with sadness rather than rushing to feel better; rewritten for permission tone

zh-tw.content:
難過不用急著趕走，
讓它待一下，它自己會知道什麼時候該走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 我們太想趕快「好起來」，反而把情緒逼到牆角，結果它賴著更久。其實難過跟客人一樣，你越想轟它走，它越不肯走。這句話想說：可以先陪著它坐一會兒。傳給那種一難過就拼命想轉念、卻越想越累的朋友。
variations: 情緒不是用力推就會走，給它一點空間反而比較快。
你可以難過，也可以同時知道它不會永遠都在。

en.content:
You don't have to rush sadness out the door.
Let it stay a while — it knows when it's time to leave.
en.editorNote: We're so eager to 'get better' that we corner our feelings, and they just dig in longer. Sadness is like a guest: the harder you try to shove it out, the more it refuses to go. This one says you're allowed to sit with it for a bit. Send it to the friend who tries to instantly reframe every bad feeling and only ends up more drained.
variations: Feelings don't leave faster when you shove them — giving them space actually works better.
You can be sad and also know it won't stay forever.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 mental-health reminder 'healing is rarely linear, you are allowed to begin again'; rewritten with permission tone, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
復原本來就不是一條直線，
你今天退回原點，也還是可以明天重新開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「明明已經好一點了，怎麼又掉回去」而自責的人。狀態起伏是正常的，不是你不夠努力。傳給最近覺得自己又退步、很挫折的朋友。
variations: 進兩步退一步，那也還是往前，沒有白走。
可以重來，而且要重來幾次都可以。

en.content:
Healing was never a straight line.
Even if you slid back to the start today, you can begin again tomorrow.
en.editorNote: For the person beating themselves up over 'I was finally better, why am I back here?' Ups and downs are normal — not proof you didn't try hard enough. Send it to a friend who feels like they've slipped backward lately.
variations: Two steps forward, one step back is still forward. None of it was wasted.
You're allowed to start over — as many times as it takes.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 wellness theme 'rest is not laziness, it is a need'; rewritten conversationally for permission-to-rest tone

zh-tw.content:
休息不是偷懶，
是你的身體在跟你說：我需要被好好對待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一停下來就有罪惡感的人。把休息當成必要，而不是獎勵。適合貼在自己工作桌前，或傳給那種累到不行還在硬撐的朋友。
variations: 休息是補給，不是欠人情，你不用為了喘口氣道歉。
你不是不夠努力才需要休息，是因為你一直很努力。

en.content:
Rest isn't laziness.
It's your body telling you: I need to be looked after.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels guilty the moment they stop. Treat rest as a need, not a reward you have to earn. Good to keep at your desk, or send to a friend who's running on empty but still pushing.
variations: Rest is refueling, not a favor you owe back. You don't have to apologize for catching your breath.
You don't need rest because you slacked off — you need it because you've been going so hard.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing theme 'difficult seasons are part of being human, not signs of failure'; rewritten for warmth, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
日子過得很辛苦，不代表你哪裡做錯了。
有時候就只是輪到你走一段比較難走的路而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一遇到低潮就先檢討自己「是不是我有問題」的人。難關不是懲罰，也不是你不夠好的證據。傳給最近卡關、很想知道「為什麼是我」的朋友。
variations: 不是你不夠好，只是這陣子真的比較難，兩件事不一樣。
難走的路也是路，走過去你還是你，而且更懂自己。

en.content:
A hard season doesn't mean you did something wrong.
Sometimes it's just your turn to walk the rougher stretch of the road.
en.editorNote: For the person whose first move in a low point is to interrogate themselves — 'is something wrong with me?' A hard time isn't a punishment or proof you're not good enough. Send it to a friend who's stuck and quietly asking 'why me?'
variations: It's not that you're not good enough — it's just genuinely hard right now. Those aren't the same thing.
The rough road is still a road. You'll come out the other side still you — and knowing yourself better.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '溫柔地把自己打醒' self-discovery theme; rewritten around the idea of moving at your own pace, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
你可以慢慢走，不用跟著世界的速度跑。
你的時區，本來就只有你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一看到別人結婚、升職、買房就開始焦慮的人。每個人的進度條長度不一樣，慢不代表落後。傳給最近一直被「同齡人都怎樣了」追著跑的朋友。
variations: 別人到站不代表你遲到，你只是還在自己的路上。
花有花的季節，你也有你的，急著開反而開不好。

en.content:
You're allowed to go slow. You don't have to keep up with the world's pace.
You were always running on your own clock.
en.editorNote: For the person who spirals every time someone else gets married, promoted, or buys a house. Everyone's timeline is a different length — slow isn't behind. Send it to a friend who's being chased by 'but everyone my age has already...'
variations: Other people arriving doesn't mean you're late. You're just still on your own road.
Flowers bloom in their own season, and so do you. Rushing it only ruins the bloom.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion theme 'be gentle with yourself on the hardest days'; rewritten for warmth, no attribution

zh-tw.content: 對別人那麼溫柔的你，也值得被自己溫柔對待一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對朋友超有耐心、對自己卻很嚴格的人。你罵自己的那些話，你捨得對朋友說嗎？傳給那個總是先照顧別人、最後才想到自己的人。
variations: 你願意原諒朋友的失誤，那也留一份給自己吧。
你對自己說話的方式，朋友聽到都會心疼。換個溫柔點的語氣吧。

en.content: You're so gentle with everyone else. You deserve to be gentle with yourself for once too.
en.editorNote: For the person who's endlessly patient with friends but brutal on themselves. The things you say to yourself — would you ever say them to a friend? Send it to someone who always takes care of everyone else first.
variations: You forgive your friends for slipping up. Save a little of that for yourself.
The way you talk to yourself would break a friend's heart to hear. Try a kinder voice.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing is a process, not a destination' theme; rewritten with everyday imagery, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
復原本來就不是一條直線。
今天退兩步沒關係，明天還是可以再往前。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種以為「好起來」就應該一天比一天好的人。狀態起起伏伏很正常，退步不等於前功盡棄。傳給最近覺得自己「又變回原樣」而沮喪的朋友。
variations: 好轉的路上本來就會反覆，這不是你不夠努力，是恢復本來的樣子。
進三步退兩步，總和還是有在往前，別只盯著退的那兩步。

en.content:
Healing was never a straight line.
If you slipped back two steps today, that's okay — tomorrow you can still move forward.
en.editorNote: For the person who assumes 'getting better' should mean improving every single day. Ups and downs are normal; a step back isn't square one. Send it to a friend who's discouraged because they feel like they 'went back to how they were.'
variations: Recovery loops back on itself — that's not you failing, that's just what healing looks like.
Three steps forward, two back still nets forward. Stop staring only at the two you lost.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the rest between two deep breaths' mindfulness theme; rewritten around permission to pause, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
有時候一整天最重要的事，
就是好好地深呼吸一口氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「今天什麼都沒做」就有罪惡感的人。光是撐過來、好好呼吸，本身就已經是一件事了。傳給把行程塞得滿滿、忘記停下來的朋友，或貼在自己最忙的那一天。
variations: 不是每一天都要有產出，有些日子能好好呼吸就夠了。
今天唯一的待辦事項：活著，順便喘口氣。其他明天再說。

en.content:
Sometimes the most important thing you do all day
is take one good, deep breath.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels guilty on a day they 'didn't get anything done.' Just making it through and breathing properly already counts as something. Send it to a friend who overpacks their schedule and forgets to pause — or keep it for your busiest day.
variations: Not every day needs output. Some days, breathing well is the whole accomplishment.
Today's only to-do: stay alive, and catch your breath. Everything else can wait until tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion theme 'turn judgment into curiosity'; rewritten around speaking kindly to yourself, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
試著用對待好朋友的口氣，
跟自己說話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對別人很寬容、對自己卻很嚴苛的人。如果這句話你不會拿來罵朋友，那也別拿來罵自己。適合傳給最近一直在自責的人。
variations: 你罵自己的那些話，捨得對朋友說嗎？捨不得的話，也放過自己吧。
對自己兇沒有比較進步，溫柔一點，你也走得動。

en.content:
Try talking to yourself
the way you'd talk to a good friend.
en.editorNote: For people who are endlessly forgiving toward others but brutal toward themselves. If you wouldn't say it to a friend, don't say it to yourself either. Send it to someone who's been stuck in self-blame lately.
variations: Would you say the things you tell yourself to your best friend? If not, let yourself off the hook too.
Being harsh with yourself doesn't make you grow faster. Be gentle — you'll still get there.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are allowed to begin again, as many times as needed'; rewritten around permission to restart, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
可以重來的，
而且想重來幾次都可以。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「都已經失敗過了，再開始也沒意義」的人。重新開始不需要理由，也不限次數。適合傳給卡在原地、不敢再試一次的朋友。
variations: 沒有規定一件事只能努力一次，今天想重來，那就重來。
你不是回到原點，你是帶著上次的經驗，再走一遍。

en.content:
You're allowed to start over —
as many times as you need.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I've already failed, so trying again is pointless.' Beginning again needs no reason and has no limit. Send it to a friend who's stuck and afraid to try one more time.
variations: There's no rule that says you only get one attempt. If today you want to start over, start over.
You're not back at zero — you're walking the same road again, but with everything you learned last time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing K-drama theme 'emotions getting sick is as normal as a cold'; rewritten, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
心情會生病，
就跟感冒一樣正常。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「心情不好是自己太脆弱」的人。情緒會感冒，需要休息和時間慢慢好，這不是你的錯。適合傳給最近狀態很低、又怪自己怎麼還沒好起來的朋友。
variations: 感冒了你會讓自己休息，心情感冒了也一樣，別急著逼自己康復。
不舒服就承認不舒服，這不是軟弱，是你在好好照顧自己。

en.content:
Feelings can get sick too —
it's as normal as catching a cold.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'feeling low means I'm weak.' Emotions can come down with something too, and they need rest and time to recover — that's not your fault. Send it to a friend who's been struggling and blaming themselves for not bouncing back yet.
variations: When you catch a cold you let yourself rest. When your heart catches one, do the same — don't rush the recovery.
Admitting you're not okay isn't weakness. It's you taking care of yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'distinguish what's worth your energy from what only drains you' (放過) theme; rewritten, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
放過，不是認輸，
是你終於分得清楚什麼值得你花力氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「放手就等於放棄」的人。把力氣留給真正重要的事，這不是逃避，是更聰明地活著。適合傳給一直在硬撐、卻被無關緊要的事消耗掉的朋友。
variations: 不是每件事都需要你拼到最後，學會放手，也是一種前進。
把力氣花在會回應你的人事物上，剩下的，輕輕放下就好。

en.content:
Letting go isn't admitting defeat.
It's finally knowing what's actually worth your energy.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels like letting go is the same as giving up. Saving your strength for what truly matters isn't avoidance — it's living smarter. Send it to a friend who keeps grinding while unimportant things drain them dry.
variations: Not everything deserves your last ounce of effort. Learning to let go is its own kind of moving forward.
Spend your energy on what answers back. The rest, set down gently.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'healing is not linear' theme; rewritten with original Taiwanese phrasing, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
復原本來就不是一條直線，
會前進，也會退回去。
退回去不代表白走，只是這次你比較認得路了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「怎麼又回到原點」而灰心的人。情緒起伏不是退步，是復原的正常樣子。適合傳給最近狀態又掉下去、開始責怪自己的朋友。
variations: 好起來這件事沒有進度條，有時候慢、有時候倒退，都還是在好起來。
你不是又壞掉了，你只是還在路上，而且這條路本來就彎彎曲曲的。

en.content:
Healing was never a straight line.
You'll move forward, and you'll slip back.
Slipping back doesn't erase the trip — it just means you know the way a little better now.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels crushed by 'why am I back at square one again.' Ups and downs aren't backsliding — they're what recovery actually looks like. Send it to a friend whose mood dipped again and who's started blaming themselves for it.
variations: Getting better has no progress bar. Sometimes it's slow, sometimes it reverses — and it's still getting better.
You're not broken again. You're still on the road, and that road was always going to be winding.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'give yourself the same kindness you'd give others' self-compassion theme; rewritten, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友從不會說「你不夠好」，
那為什麼對自己這麼狠？
把你給別人的那份體貼，留一份給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種對別人超有耐心、對自己卻特別嚴苛的人。提醒對方，自己也值得那份溫柔，不是只有別人才配。卡在自我苛責的時候讀一下。
variations: 如果這句話是朋友說的，你早就抱抱他了，那也抱抱自己吧。
對自己講話之前，先想想：這句話你捨得對朋友說嗎？

en.content:
You'd never tell a friend 'you're not good enough.'
So why say it to yourself?
Save a little of the kindness you hand out so freely.
en.editorNote: For the person who's endlessly patient with everyone else but brutal toward themselves. A reminder that they deserve that gentleness too — it's not reserved for other people. Read it when you're stuck in self-criticism.
variations: If a friend said this out loud, you'd have hugged them already. So hug yourself.
Before you talk to yourself, ask: would you ever say this to someone you love?

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the rest between two breaths' self-care theme; rewritten with original imagery, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
有時候一整天裡最重要的事，
就是兩次深呼吸之間，
那短短一秒鐘的休息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「沒空休息」的人。休息不一定要請假或放長假，光是好好呼吸一下就算。適合傳給行程滿到連喘氣都覺得奢侈的朋友。
variations: 你不需要一個完整的假期才能休息，現在這一口氣就可以。
把肩膀放下來，吐一口氣，這也是在照顧自己。

en.content:
Sometimes the most important thing all day
is that tiny pause
between one deep breath and the next.
en.editorNote: For the person who insists they 'have no time to rest.' Rest doesn't require a day off or a long vacation — one good breath counts. Send it to a friend whose schedule is so packed that even exhaling feels like a luxury.
variations: You don't need a whole vacation to rest. This one breath, right now, will do.
Drop your shoulders, let one breath out. That's self-care too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '情緒生病就跟感冒一樣正常' healing-drama theme; rewritten, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
情緒感冒了，跟身體感冒一樣正常。
沒有人會罵你「怎麼又感冒了」，
那也別這樣罵你的心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種因為「又低潮了」而自責的人。情緒會生病很正常，需要的是休養不是責備。適合傳給最近狀態不好、卻覺得自己很糟糕的朋友。
variations: 心情會感冒，會發燒，也會慢慢好，給它一點時間。
你不是抗壓性差，你只是現在剛好在生病，生病的人需要被照顧。

en.content:
Your feelings can catch a cold, same as your body.
Nobody scolds you for getting sick —
so don't scold your heart for it either.
en.editorNote: For the person who beats themselves up for 'being down again.' Emotions getting sick is normal; what they need is rest, not blame. Send it to a friend who's struggling lately but thinks that makes them a failure.
variations: Moods can come down with something, run a fever, and slowly recover too. Give it time.
You're not weak. You're just a little sick right now, and sick people deserve care.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '學會與自己溫柔相處' single/self-companionship theme; rewritten, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
學會一個人好好相處，
不是因為沒人陪，
是因為你終於成為自己最安心的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種害怕獨處的人。獨處不等於孤單，能跟自己自在共處其實是一種很穩的力量。適合傳給最近一個人生活、偶爾覺得空空的朋友。
variations: 一個人也能很好，不是逞強，是你跟自己處得來。
最該先學會喜歡你的人，是你自己。

en.content:
Learning to be at ease on your own
isn't because no one's around —
it's because you've finally become the person you feel safest with.
en.editorNote: For the person who's afraid of being alone. Solitude isn't the same as loneliness; being comfortable in your own company is a quiet, steady kind of strength. Send it to a friend living solo who feels a little hollow some days.
variations: Being okay alone isn't toughing it out — it's getting along well with yourself.
The first person who needs to learn to like you is you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '用最溫柔的方式把自己打醒' self-exploration theme; rewritten, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
你不用急著把情緒整理好，
先讓自己難過一下，
它需要的不是答案，是被看見。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一有情緒就想趕快壓下去的人。很多時候情緒不是要被解決，只是想被承認一下。適合傳給最近在硬撐、假裝沒事的朋友。
variations: 情緒不是麻煩，是它在提醒你，有些事你還在乎。
先允許自己感覺，再慢慢處理，順序不要顛倒。

en.content:
You don't have to tidy up your feelings right away.
Let yourself be sad for a bit first —
what it needs isn't an answer, it's to be seen.
en.editorNote: For the person who tries to shove every feeling down the second it shows up. A lot of the time emotions don't need solving — they just want to be acknowledged. Send it to a friend who's white-knuckling through and pretending they're fine.
variations: Your feelings aren't a nuisance — they're reminding you that you still care about something.
Let yourself feel it first, then deal with it. Don't flip the order.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '40句心情語錄' gentle-mood theme; original writing on worries shrinking by morning

zh-tw.content:
那些半夜把你壓得喘不過氣的事，
常常到了早上，
會發現它沒有想像中那麼大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜的腦袋最會把事情放大。這句話適合在睡前傳給容易胡思亂想的朋友，提醒他先睡，明天的事明天再說。
variations: 凌晨三點的煩惱有八成是假的，先睡，醒來再看一次。
撐過今晚，明天的你會比現在更有力氣面對。

en.content:
The things that crush the breath out of you at midnight
often turn out, by morning,
to be smaller than they seemed.
en.editorNote: A late-night brain is an expert at blowing things up. Good to send before bed to a friend who spirals — a nudge to sleep first and let tomorrow handle tomorrow.
variations: Eighty percent of 3am worries are fiction. Sleep first, look again when you wake up.
Just get through tonight. Tomorrow-you will have more in the tank than you do right now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion 'be gentle with yourself, doing your best' theme; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
你對別人那麼溫柔，
也記得留一點溫柔給自己。
你也是那個值得被好好對待的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人對別人很體貼，對自己卻特別嚴格。這句話是個提醒：你給出去的溫柔，自己也有資格收下一份。適合傳給總是先想到別人的朋友。
variations: 你罵自己的那些話，捨得對朋友說嗎？不捨得的話，也別對自己說。
對自己好一點，不是放縱，是你也需要被善待。

en.content:
You're so gentle with everyone else —
remember to save a little of that for yourself.
You're someone who deserves to be treated well too.
en.editorNote: So many people are tender with others and brutal with themselves. This is a reminder that the kindness you hand out, you're allowed to keep some of. Send it to a friend who always puts everyone else first.
variations: Would you say the things you say to yourself to a friend? If not, stop saying them to you.
Being kind to yourself isn't indulgence — you need to be treated well too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '現代人最需要的療癒金句' time-zone metaphor; rewritten, no attribution

zh-tw.content:
別人的進度不是你的時鐘，
你有你自己的時區，
沒有遲到，也沒有錯過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看到同齡的人結婚、升遷、買房，難免會焦慮自己是不是太慢。這句話是個解套，提醒你人生不是賽跑。適合傳給最近被「同儕壓力」困住的朋友。
variations: 花有花期，你也有，急著開反而開不好。
你不是落後，只是還沒輪到你的那一頁。

en.content:
Other people's progress isn't your clock.
You're in your own time zone —
you're not late, and you haven't missed anything.
en.editorNote: Watching people your age get married, promoted, buy houses — it's hard not to wonder if you're falling behind. This unhooks that: life isn't a race. Send it to a friend stuck under peer pressure.
variations: Flowers have their season. So do you. Rushing the bloom just ruins it.
You're not behind — your page just hasn't come up yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing-quotes 'healing isn't linear' theme; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
復原不是一條直線，
有時候會退回去一點，
那不是失敗，是它本來的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 走出低潮的路常常是進兩步退一步，退回去的那天最容易自責。這句話是想說：那很正常。適合傳給正在慢慢好起來、偶爾又掉下去的朋友。
variations: 今天比昨天難過，不代表你白走了，路本來就有上有下。
好轉的過程是鋸齒狀的，不是滑梯，能繼續往前就夠了。

en.content:
Healing isn't a straight line.
Sometimes you slide back a little —
that's not failure, that's just how it works.
en.editorNote: Climbing out of a low spot is usually two steps forward, one step back, and the back days are when you beat yourself up the most. This says: that's normal. Send it to a friend who's slowly getting better but dips now and then.
variations: A harder day than yesterday doesn't mean you've undone the progress. The path has ups and downs by design.
Getting better is a jagged line, not a slide. Still moving forward is enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion / inner-critic theme; rewritten as permission to speak kindly to oneself

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友那麼溫柔，
為什麼輪到自己就那麼兇？
換個語氣跟自己說話吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 很多人對別人的失誤很包容，對自己卻一句都不放過。這句話是想提醒：你也值得被自己好好對待。適合傳給那種總是先檢討自己的朋友。
variations: 如果朋友犯了這個錯，你會這樣罵他嗎？不會的話，也別這樣罵自己。
對自己說話的口氣，慢慢就變成你看自己的樣子。

en.content:
You're so gentle with your friends —
why do you turn so harsh the moment it's you?
Try talking to yourself in a kinder voice.
en.editorNote: A lot of people forgive everyone but themselves. This is a reminder that you deserve the same gentleness you hand out so freely. Good for the friend who always blames themselves first.
variations: If a friend made this mistake, would you talk to them this way? If not, don't talk to yourself that way either.
The tone you use on yourself slowly becomes the way you see yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you're allowed to begin again' theme; rewritten on restarting without shame

zh-tw.content:
重來一次不丟臉，
你想重來幾次都可以。
沒人規定一次就要成功。
zh-tw.editorNote: 戒了又破、計畫又停、習慣又斷掉的時候最容易放棄。這句話是想說：重啟不是退步。適合傳給正在第N次嘗試的朋友，或貼給自己看。
variations: 今天又從頭開始，不代表前面都白費，只是你還沒放棄。
計畫被打斷不等於失敗，重新接上就好。

en.content:
Starting over isn't embarrassing.
You can begin again as many times as you need.
Nobody said you had to get it right on the first try.
en.editorNote: Quitting feels closest right after you break a streak, drop a plan, or fall off a habit. This says: restarting isn't going backward. Good for a friend on their Nth attempt — or for your own desktop.
variations: Starting from scratch today doesn't erase everything before it. It just means you haven't given up.
An interrupted plan isn't a failed one. Just pick it back up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'walking out at your own pace' theme; rewritten on permission to move slowly

zh-tw.content:
走出來不容易，
用你自己的速度就好。
沒有人在後面追你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 別人問「你怎麼還沒好」的時候，最傷。這句話是想拿掉那個倒數計時，讓人按自己的節奏慢慢來。適合傳給正在復原、卻被催著快點好的朋友。
variations: 好起來沒有時間表，你不是落後，只是還在路上。
別人走多快是別人的事，你的步調由你自己決定。

en.content:
Getting through it isn't easy.
Go at your own pace.
There's no one chasing you from behind.
en.editorNote: The question 'why aren't you over it yet?' stings the most. This removes the countdown and lets you heal on your own clock. Good for a friend who's recovering but being rushed to hurry up.
variations: Healing has no timetable. You're not behind — you're still on your way.
How fast anyone else walks is their business. Your pace is yours to set.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'to my future self, I hope you wake up lighter' theme; rewritten as a gentle wish

zh-tw.content:
希望有一天醒來，
你會覺得肩膀輕了一點，
心裡的那塊石頭，悄悄被搬走了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 不是叫人立刻好起來，而是給一個溫柔的盼望：總有一天會輕鬆一點。適合在對方很累的時候傳過去，當作一句不催促的祝福。
variations: 願你某個早晨睜開眼，發現昨天那麼重的事，今天沒那麼重了。
你現在背的東西，不會永遠那麼重，相信它會慢慢變輕。

en.content:
I hope one day you wake up
and your shoulders feel a little lighter —
that the heavy stone in your chest got quietly carried away.
en.editorNote: Not a push to feel better right now, but a gentle wish that someday it'll get easier. Good to send when someone's worn out — a blessing that doesn't rush them.
variations: May there be a morning when you open your eyes and yesterday's weight just isn't as heavy.
What you're carrying now won't always be this heavy. Trust that it lightens, little by little.
